- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings ŌåÆ Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch ŌĆö but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email ŌåÆ creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge ŌĆö but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" ŌĆö no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.

## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)

- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` ŌĆö the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design ┬¦3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.

## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)

Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.

- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** ŌĆö when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** ŌĆö when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** ŌĆö only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** ŌĆö when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** ŌĆö explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** ŌĆö Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

I'm debugging a bug in the ax-next monorepo (pnpm workspace, working dir is a git worktree at /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/fix-ui-bugs). 

BUG: "Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats." When a user starts a new chat, a human-readable title should be auto-generated and shown in the sidebar, but sometimes it isn't ŌĆö it stays as a default/placeholder or remains blank inconsistently.

Do a "very thorough" investigation. I need you to find the ROOT CAUSE, not just where the code lives. Specifically trace:

1. The `@ax/conversation-titles` plugin (likely packages/conversation-titles/). Read its plugin.ts fully. How and when does it generate a title? It fires on `chat:turn-end`. Under what exact conditions does it generate vs skip? (e.g. only on first turn? checks if title already set? min turn count?)

2. How does it call the LLM? It reads `settings:fast-model` storage, parses `provider/model-id`, routes through `llm:call:<provider>`, falls back to `cfg.model`. What happens on error / when the model is not registered / when storage is missing? Does it fail silently? Is there a swallowed catch that would explain "inconsistent" generation?

3. Where is the generated title persisted, and how does channel-web sidebar read it? Find the conversations list/sidebar read path. Is there a race ŌĆö e.g. the sidebar reads the conversation list BEFORE the title is generated (title generation is async post-turn-end), so a freshly-created chat shows no title until refresh?

4. Is title generation idempotent/retried? If the first turn-end fails to produce a title, does a later turn ever retry, or is it one-shot?

5. Check for any condition like "only generate if turn count == 1" or "skip if title already non-null" that combined with a race or error could cause inconsistency.

6. Look at how new conversations get their initial title (find-or-create conversation path). What's the default title for a brand-new chat? Is there a fallback to first-message-text anywhere?

Look at tests in conversation-titles/__tests__ to understand intended behavior. Also grep channel-web for how the sidebar/conversation-list renders titles and whether it polls or refreshes.

Report back: (a) the precise root cause(s) of the inconsistency with file:line citations, (b) the conditions under which a title is and isn't generated, (c) the error-handling behavior, (d) a recommended fix approach. Be concrete with file paths and line numbers. Do NOT modify any files.

I'm debugging a bug in the ax-next monorepo (pnpm workspace, working dir is a git worktree at /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/fix-ui-bugs).

BUG: When a user attaches a file to a message (e.g. attaches a PDF and types "Summarize this file"), the message shows an attachment chip correctly while in the live session. BUT when that same chat is later loaded from a previous chat session via the sidebar (cold history load), the attachment chip does NOT render. Instead, raw text is displayed:
  "Summarize this file\nUser attached 'Disaster Recovery Plan - v3.pdf' at .ax/uploads/cnv_HqkVq7CQogkmnXBrRFcTpg/req-d194ad9d37af/a60d0978__Disaster_Recovery_Plan_-_v3.pdf (application/pdf)"

That "User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)" string is the runner-side attachment-translation provenance/text-mention preamble. So my hypothesis is: the conversation history read path now reads the runner's native jsonl transcript (runner-owned-sessions design), and the jsonl contains the TRANSLATED content the SDK saw (text + text-mention) rather than the original `attachment` ContentBlock. So the original attachment block is lost on history load and only the translated text survives ŌĆö hence no chip.

Do a "very thorough" investigation to CONFIRM or REFUTE this hypothesis and find the exact root cause. Trace these paths:

1. The attachment-translation pass in `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/attachment-translation.ts). Confirm the exact format of the text mention string. Per memory, for application/pdf it produces either a `document` block (if AX_SDK_DOCUMENT_BLOCKS=1 / SUPPORTS_DOCUMENT_BLOCKS) OR a text mention "User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)". Confirm.

2. How does the chat-messages handler store/forward attachments? Find packages/channel-web POST /api/chat/messages handler and how `attachment_ref` becomes an `attachment` ContentBlock via `attachments:commit`. What gets sent to the runner ŌĆö the original ContentBlock[] with the attachment block, or the translated text? Where does translation happen (runner-side, per memory)?

3. THE KEY QUESTION: What does the conversation HISTORY READ path return? Find `conversations:get` (likely packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts). Does it read the runner's jsonl transcript (.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl) via workspace:read? Parse what content/contentBlocks it returns for a user message that had an attachment. Does the original `attachment` block survive in the jsonl, or only the SDK-translated text? This is the crux ŌĆö find where the attachment block is lost.

4. The channel-web render path. Find UserFilePart / AttachmentChip / MessagePrimitive.Parts.components.File and how a loaded message maps content/contentBlocks ŌåÆ rendered parts. Per PR #97 memory, "the history-load path (cold reload) works correctly through UserFilePart" ŌĆö but that was BEFORE runner-owned-sessions cutover. Check whether the message adapter that converts stored conversation turns ŌåÆ assistant-ui messages still produces file parts, or whether it only gets plain text now.

5. Trace how a stored turn's contentBlocks flow from conversations:get ŌåÆ channel-web conversation GET route ŌåÆ the browser message list ŌåÆ assistant-ui. Where would an `attachment` block become visible as a chip vs render as text? Find the adapter (AxAttachmentAdapter or a convertMessage / message-converter).

6. Check git log / recent changes to conversations plugin and the jsonl parsing to see if Phase D/E of runner-owned-sessions changed conversations:get to read jsonl instead of the host conversation_turns table.

Report back: (a) CONFIRM or REFUTE the hypothesis with evidence, (b) the EXACT point where the original attachment block is lost on history load (file:line), (c) whether the jsonl transcript preserves attachment blocks or only translated text, (d) what the channel-web render path receives for a historical attachment message, (e) a recommended fix approach (e.g. store/restore attachment blocks on the host side keyed by turn, or reconstruct chips from the text-mention, or persist original blocks separately). Be concrete with file paths and line numbers. Do NOT modify any files.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document:

## ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS: Chat Session Title Generation Bug

Based on my thorough investigation, I've identified **multiple root causes** contributing to the inconsistent title generation. Here's the detailed analysis:

---

### **1. PRIMARY ROOT CAUSE: Race Condition Between Title Generation and Sidebar Read**

**The Core Issue:**
When a new chat is created and the user sends their first message, the following sequence occurs:

1. **User sends message** ŌåÆ `/api/chat/messages` POST creates conversation via `conversations:create` 
   - Conversation is created with `title = null` (passed as undefined, defaulting to null)
   - `/api/chat/messages` returns `202 Accepted` with `conversationId`
2. **Browser receives response** and calls `generateTitle(conversationId)` in `thread-list-adapter.ts:68`
3. **Simultaneously**, on the backend:
   - `agent:invoke` is fired (async, not awaited) at `/api/chat/messages:515`
   - The runner processes the first turn and emits `chat:turn-end` event
   - `@ax/conversation-titles` plugin's subscriber fires on that event
   - Title LLM is called, result validated, then `conversations:set-title` is called

4. **The Race**: The browser's `generateTitle()` polls `/api/chat/conversations` starting at line 74-75 (1-second sleep, then poll)
   - If the backend title generation is **still in progress** or **hasn't started yet**, the poll returns `title: null`
   - After 3 attempts (3 seconds), it gives up and uses fallback `"New Chat"`
   - **Meanwhile**, the backend might successfully generate the title 4+ seconds later, but the UI already settled on "New Chat"

**File Citations:**
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:68-98` ŌĆö The polling logic with hard-coded 3 attempts at 1-second intervals (max 3-second wait)
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:495-522` ŌĆö Fire-and-forget `agent:invoke` dispatch (NOT awaited); 202 returned immediately
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:133-162` ŌĆö Subscriber registered, fires on `chat:turn-end`

---

### **2. SECONDARY ROOT CAUSE: Transient LLM Failures Silently Fail With No Retry**

**The Issue:**
Once the first assistant turn fires the title-generation attempt, if **anything fails** (LLM timeout, network error, validation rejection), the title is **never retried**.

**Exact Failure Modes:**

1. **LLM Call Fails** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:293-303`)
   - If `bus.call(..., 'llm:call:anthropic')` throws a `PluginError`, it's caught in the try-catch at line 153-159
   - Error is logged at WARN level but execution returns undefined at line 161
   - `conversations:set-title` is **never called** ŌåÆ title stays `NULL`

2. **Validation Rejects** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322`)
   - If model returns empty, "Untitled", or pure whitespace (per `validateGeneratedTitle` in `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts:26-43`)
   - `title === null` triggers line 306 return
   - `conversations:set-title` is **never called** ŌåÆ title stays `NULL`

3. **Only ONE Attempt, No Retry Loop**
   - `handleTurnEnd` checks `if (assistantTurnCount !== 1) return;` at line 290
   - If first attempt fails, second+ assistant turns **explicitly skip** re-titling by design (lines 281-286)
   - Comment: "we deliberately don't retry on later turns"

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:143-161` ŌĆö Subscriber catches errors, logs, continues without throwing
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:287-290` ŌĆö Explicit check: only fires on FIRST assistant turn; skips on 2nd+
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322` ŌĆö Validation failure silently returns (title remains null)
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts:37-40` ŌĆö Rejects empty, "Untitled", whitespace

---

### **3. STORAGE RESOLUTION ISSUES: Model Override May Fallback Silently**

**The Issue:**
The title generation attempts to read a runtime model override from `settings:fast-model` storage. Multiple scenarios cause it to silently fall back to the configured default without clear indication:

1. **Storage Layer Not Registered** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:202`)
   - If `storage:get` hook is not registered, `resolveTitleModel()` returns fallback result
   - No error logged; silent fallback

2. **Storage Read Fails** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:204-214`)
   - Transient backend issue ŌåÆ logged at DEBUG level only
   - Silently falls back; user never sees why title generation used a different model than expected

3. **Invalid Model Reference in Storage** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:224-235`)
   - Value is not a valid `provider/model-id` format ŌåÆ logged at WARN level
   - Silently falls back to configured model

4. **Override Provider Hook Not Registered** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:237-246`)
   - Storage says "use openai/gpt-4o" but only anthropic hook is wired ŌåÆ logged at WARN
   - Falls back to configured anthropic hook; title generation still proceeds but uses wrong model

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:189-252` ŌĆö `resolveTitleModel()` function with multiple fallback paths
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:202` ŌĆö Check for storage service registration
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:210-214` ŌĆö Catch and log storage errors
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:228-234` ŌĆö Invalid ref detection
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:241-246` ŌĆö Override hook missing detection

---

### **4. IDEMPOTENCY PROTECTION DOESN'T ACCOUNT FOR LATE ARRIVALS**

**The Issue:**
The `ifNull: true` guard on `conversations:set-title` (line 331) correctly prevents double-titles but doesn't prevent the scenario where:
1. First title generation attempt fails (LLM error, validation rejected)
2. Title stays `NULL`
3. User manually renames (sets title)
4. A **duplicate** `chat:turn-end` event arrives (at-least-once delivery)
5. The late subscriber attempts to generate and set a title again
6. `ifNull: true` prevents overwrite (correct behavior), but the earlier failure is never recovered

The issue is that there's no **automatic retry** for the initial failureŌĆöonce the first assistant turn fires, all subsequent turns are skipped by design.

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:104-106` ŌĆö Idempotency comment
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:331` ŌĆö `ifNull: true` contract
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:334-341` ŌĆö Race handling (debug log on `updated: false`)

---

### **5. TIMING WINDOW: New Chat Shows as "Untitled" if Turn Ends Before Title Persists**

**The Scenario:**
1. Conversation created with `title = null`
2. UI calls `generateTitle()` which starts polling
3. Backend fires `chat:turn-end` on assistant response
4. Title generation completes and calls `conversations:set-title`
5. **BUT** if title-setting takes >3 seconds total (LLM latency), all 3 UI polls will see `title: null`

The frontend doesn't have a mechanism to **refresh the title after the initial 3 polls**. Once `generateTitle()` gives up, the sidebar shows "New Chat" forever, even if the title eventually persists on the backend.

**File Citations:**
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:73-93` ŌĆö Hardcoded 3 attempts, then fallback with no further polling
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:526-564` ŌĆö `listConversations` always returns current DB state; no background polling from UI side

---

### **6. INITIAL TITLE STATE: Always NULL Until First Turn**

**The Behavior:**
- `conversations:create` accepts an optional `title` parameter (see `packages/conversations/src/types.ts:108-120`)
- When title is undefined/null, it's stored as `NULL` in the database
- No fallback to first-message text or placeholder is implemented
- The conversation list will show `title: null` until the first assistant turn completes **and** the title LLM succeeds

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversations/src/types.ts:108-120` ŌĆö `CreateInput.title?: string | null`
- `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:534` ŌĆö `validateTitle(input.title ?? null)` defaults to null
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:295-298` ŌĆö No title passed to `conversations:create`

---

## **SUMMARY: Root Causes**

| # | Root Cause | Condition | Result |
|---|-----------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | **Race between title generation and UI polling** | Title LLM takes >3 seconds | UI polls 3x at 1-second intervals, gives up, shows "New Chat" while backend still generating |
| 2 | **LLM errors silent, no retry** | `llm:call:anthropic` fails | Error logged (WARN), subscriber continues, title stays NULL, never retried on later turns |
| 3 | **Validation rejection silent, no retry** | Model outputs "Untitled" or empty | Logged (DEBUG), title stays NULL, never retried |
| 4 | **One-shot title generation** | First assistant turn fires title generation | If it fails, 2nd+ assistant turns **explicitly skip** re-titling by design (line 290 check) |
| 5 | **Storage resolution fallback silent** | Storage not registered or invalid model ref | Falls back to configured model without UI indication; model mismatch never surfaced |
| 6 | **No title refresh after polling gives up** | Polling times out | UI shows "New Chat" forever; no mechanism to refresh after initial 3-second wait |
| 7 | **Empty initial title** | New conversation created | `title = null` until first assistant turn **and** title generation succeeds |

---

## **RECOMMENDED FIX APPROACH**

### **Short-term fixes (high impact, low complexity):**

1. **Increase polling timeout in thread-list-adapter** 
   - Change `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:74-75` from 3 attempts to 5-6 attempts, or increase interval from 1s to 2s
   - Gives backend more time to complete title generation before UI gives up
   - **Trade-off**: Slightly slower "New Chat" fallback on LLM failure, but more likely to catch successful generation

2. **Add retry mechanism to conversation-titles plugin**
   - Track failed title generations (conversation_id ŌåÆ failure reason)
   - On subsequent turns beyond the 2nd assistant turn, retry if the title is still NULL
   - Removes the hard "only try once" constraint (line 290)
   - **Requires schema change** to track retry state (or use a separate store)

3. **Surface storage/model resolution issues in logs**
   - Change `resolveTitleModel()` fallback logs from DEBUG to INFO/WARN
   - Helps operators debug why expected models aren't being used

### **Medium-term fixes (correctness):**

4. **Implement title refresh polling on the UI side**
   - After initial `generateTitle()` polling gives up, don't immediately show "New Chat"
   - Keep polling in background at lower frequency (every 5-10 seconds) until title arrives or user navigates
   - Show loading state instead of "New Chat" while waiting

5. **Make title generation idempotent with a retry budget**
   - Track assistant turn count where title was attempted
   - Allow re-attempts up to turn 3 or 4 (not just turn 1)
   - Document the intent: "We only generate once, by design, to avoid LLM cost explosion"

6. **Add fallback title generation from first user message**
   - If auto-title fails after turn 1, extract first N words from user's message as a fallback
   - Ensures conversation always has **some** title, even if LLM fails
   - Implement in `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322`

---

## **EXACT ROOT CAUSE FOR THE BUG REPORT**

**"Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats"** is caused by:

1. **Primary**: Race condition where title generation takes >3 seconds, but UI polling gives up after 3 seconds ŌåÆ shows "New Chat" while backend still working
2. **Contributing**: LLM errors and validation rejections silently fail with no automatic retry; once first turn fails, there's no second chance
3. **Contributing**: No fallback when title generation fails; conversation remains untitled forever

The inconsistency occurs because:
- **Fast LLM responses** (< 3 seconds): Title generates successfully, shows on sidebar
- **Slow LLM responses** (> 3 seconds): UI polls timeout, shows "New Chat", but backend eventually generates real title (user sees inconsistency on refresh)
- **LLM errors**: Error logged, title stays NULL forever, sidebar shows empty/placeholder

---

## **KEY FILE REFERENCES**

| File | Key Lines | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts` | 254-342 | `handleTurnEnd()` ŌĆö the core title generation logic; checks `assistantTurnCount !== 1` at line 290 |
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts` | 189-252 | `resolveTitleModel()` ŌĆö fallback logic for storage, model resolution |
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts` | 26-43 | `validateGeneratedTitle()` ŌĆö rejects empty, "Untitled", whitespace |
| `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts` | 68-98 | `generateTitle()` ŌĆö UI polling with 3 attempts at 1-second intervals |
| `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts` | 291-522 | `postMessage()` ŌĆö creates conversation, dispatches agent:invoke (fire-and-forget, line 515) |
| `packages/conversations/src/store.ts` | 610-629 | `setTitle()` ŌĆö implements `ifNull: true` compare-and-set logic |

Is there a race ŌĆö e.g. the sidebar reads the conversation list BEFORE the title is generated (title generation is async post-turn-end), so a freshly-created chat shows no title until refresh?

4. Is title generation idempotent/retried? If the first turn-end fails to produce a title, does a later turn ever retry, or is it one-shot?

5. Check for any condition like "only generate if turn count == 1" or "skip if title already non-null" that combined with a race or error could cause inconsistency.

6. Look at how new conversations get their initial title (find-or-create conversation path). What's the default title for a brand-new chat? Is there a fallback to first-message-text anywhere?

Look at tests in conversation-titles/__tests__ to understand intended behavior. Also grep channel-web for how the sidebar/conversation-list renders titles and whether it polls or refreshes.

Report back: (a) the precise root cause(s) of the inconsistency with file:line citations, (b) the conditions under which a title is and isn't generated, (c) the error-handling behavior, (d) a recommended fix approach. Be concrete with file paths and line numbers. Do NOT modify any files.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document:

## ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS: Chat Session Title Generation Bug

Based on my thorough investigation, I've identified **multiple root causes** contributing to the inconsistent title generation. Here's the detailed analysis:

---

### **1. PRIMARY ROOT CAUSE: Race Condition Between Title Generation and Sidebar Read**

**The Core Issue:**
When a new chat is created and the user sends their first message, the following sequence occurs:

1. **User sends message** ŌåÆ `/api/chat/messages` POST creates conversation via `conversations:create` 
   - Conversation is created with `title = null` (passed as undefined, defaulting to null)
   - `/api/chat/messages` returns `202 Accepted` with `conversationId`
2. **Browser receives response** and calls `generateTitle(conversationId)` in `thread-list-adapter.ts:68`
3. **Simultaneously**, on the backend:
   - `agent:invoke` is fired (async, not awaited) at `/api/chat/messages:515`
   - The runner processes the first turn and emits `chat:turn-end` event
   - `@ax/conversation-titles` plugin's subscriber fires on that event
   - Title LLM is called, result validated, then `conversations:set-title` is called

4. **The Race**: The browser's `generateTitle()` polls `/api/chat/conversations` starting at line 74-75 (1-second sleep, then poll)
   - If the backend title generation is **still in progress** or **hasn't started yet**, the poll returns `title: null`
   - After 3 attempts (3 seconds), it gives up and uses fallback `"New Chat"`
   - **Meanwhile**, the backend might successfully generate the title 4+ seconds later, but the UI already settled on "New Chat"

**File Citations:**
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:68-98` ŌĆö The polling logic with hard-coded 3 attempts at 1-second intervals (max 3-second wait)
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:495-522` ŌĆö Fire-and-forget `agent:invoke` dispatch (NOT awaited); 202 returned immediately
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:133-162` ŌĆö Subscriber registered, fires on `chat:turn-end`

---

### **2. SECONDARY ROOT CAUSE: Transient LLM Failures Silently Fail With No Retry**

**The Issue:**
Once the first assistant turn fires the title-generation attempt, if **anything fails** (LLM timeout, network error, validation rejection), the title is **never retried**.

**Exact Failure Modes:**

1. **LLM Call Fails** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:293-303`)
   - If `bus.call(..., 'llm:call:anthropic')` throws a `PluginError`, it's caught in the try-catch at line 153-159
   - Error is logged at WARN level but execution returns undefined at line 161
   - `conversations:set-title` is **never called** ŌåÆ title stays `NULL`

2. **Validation Rejects** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322`)
   - If model returns empty, "Untitled", or pure whitespace (per `validateGeneratedTitle` in `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts:26-43`)
   - `title === null` triggers line 306 return
   - `conversations:set-title` is **never called** ŌåÆ title stays `NULL`

3. **Only ONE Attempt, No Retry Loop**
   - `handleTurnEnd` checks `if (assistantTurnCount !== 1) return;` at line 290
   - If first attempt fails, second+ assistant turns **explicitly skip** re-titling by design (lines 281-286)
   - Comment: "we deliberately don't retry on later turns"

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:143-161` ŌĆö Subscriber catches errors, logs, continues without throwing
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:287-290` ŌĆö Explicit check: only fires on FIRST assistant turn; skips on 2nd+
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322` ŌĆö Validation failure silently returns (title remains null)
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts:37-40` ŌĆö Rejects empty, "Untitled", whitespace

---

### **3. STORAGE RESOLUTION ISSUES: Model Override May Fallback Silently**

**The Issue:**
The title generation attempts to read a runtime model override from `settings:fast-model` storage. Multiple scenarios cause it to silently fall back to the configured default without clear indication:

1. **Storage Layer Not Registered** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:202`)
   - If `storage:get` hook is not registered, `resolveTitleModel()` returns fallback result
   - No error logged; silent fallback

2. **Storage Read Fails** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:204-214`)
   - Transient backend issue ŌåÆ logged at DEBUG level only
   - Silently falls back; user never sees why title generation used a different model than expected

3. **Invalid Model Reference in Storage** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:224-235`)
   - Value is not a valid `provider/model-id` format ŌåÆ logged at WARN level
   - Silently falls back to configured model

4. **Override Provider Hook Not Registered** (`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:237-246`)
   - Storage says "use openai/gpt-4o" but only anthropic hook is wired ŌåÆ logged at WARN
   - Falls back to configured anthropic hook; title generation still proceeds but uses wrong model

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:189-252` ŌĆö `resolveTitleModel()` function with multiple fallback paths
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:202` ŌĆö Check for storage service registration
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:210-214` ŌĆö Catch and log storage errors
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:228-234` ŌĆö Invalid ref detection
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:241-246` ŌĆö Override hook missing detection

---

### **4. IDEMPOTENCY PROTECTION DOESN'T ACCOUNT FOR LATE ARRIVALS**

**The Issue:**
The `ifNull: true` guard on `conversations:set-title` (line 331) correctly prevents double-titles but doesn't prevent the scenario where:
1. First title generation attempt fails (LLM error, validation rejected)
2. Title stays `NULL`
3. User manually renames (sets title)
4. A **duplicate** `chat:turn-end` event arrives (at-least-once delivery)
5. The late subscriber attempts to generate and set a title again
6. `ifNull: true` prevents overwrite (correct behavior), but the earlier failure is never recovered

The issue is that there's no **automatic retry** for the initial failureŌĆöonce the first assistant turn fires, all subsequent turns are skipped by design.

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:104-106` ŌĆö Idempotency comment
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:331` ŌĆö `ifNull: true` contract
- `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:334-341` ŌĆö Race handling (debug log on `updated: false`)

---

### **5. TIMING WINDOW: New Chat Shows as "Untitled" if Turn Ends Before Title Persists**

**The Scenario:**
1. Conversation created with `title = null`
2. UI calls `generateTitle()` which starts polling
3. Backend fires `chat:turn-end` on assistant response
4. Title generation completes and calls `conversations:set-title`
5. **BUT** if title-setting takes >3 seconds total (LLM latency), all 3 UI polls will see `title: null`

The frontend doesn't have a mechanism to **refresh the title after the initial 3 polls**. Once `generateTitle()` gives up, the sidebar shows "New Chat" forever, even if the title eventually persists on the backend.

**File Citations:**
- `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:73-93` ŌĆö Hardcoded 3 attempts, then fallback with no further polling
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:526-564` ŌĆö `listConversations` always returns current DB state; no background polling from UI side

---

### **6. INITIAL TITLE STATE: Always NULL Until First Turn**

**The Behavior:**
- `conversations:create` accepts an optional `title` parameter (see `packages/conversations/src/types.ts:108-120`)
- When title is undefined/null, it's stored as `NULL` in the database
- No fallback to first-message text or placeholder is implemented
- The conversation list will show `title: null` until the first assistant turn completes **and** the title LLM succeeds

**File Citations:**
- `packages/conversations/src/types.ts:108-120` ŌĆö `CreateInput.title?: string | null`
- `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:534` ŌĆö `validateTitle(input.title ?? null)` defaults to null
- `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:295-298` ŌĆö No title passed to `conversations:create`

---

## **SUMMARY: Root Causes**

| # | Root Cause | Condition | Result |
|---|-----------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | **Race between title generation and UI polling** | Title LLM takes >3 seconds | UI polls 3x at 1-second intervals, gives up, shows "New Chat" while backend still generating |
| 2 | **LLM errors silent, no retry** | `llm:call:anthropic` fails | Error logged (WARN), subscriber continues, title stays NULL, never retried on later turns |
| 3 | **Validation rejection silent, no retry** | Model outputs "Untitled" or empty | Logged (DEBUG), title stays NULL, never retried |
| 4 | **One-shot title generation** | First assistant turn fires title generation | If it fails, 2nd+ assistant turns **explicitly skip** re-titling by design (line 290 check) |
| 5 | **Storage resolution fallback silent** | Storage not registered or invalid model ref | Falls back to configured model without UI indication; model mismatch never surfaced |
| 6 | **No title refresh after polling gives up** | Polling times out | UI shows "New Chat" forever; no mechanism to refresh after initial 3-second wait |
| 7 | **Empty initial title** | New conversation created | `title = null` until first assistant turn **and** title generation succeeds |

---

## **RECOMMENDED FIX APPROACH**

### **Short-term fixes (high impact, low complexity):**

1. **Increase polling timeout in thread-list-adapter** 
   - Change `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:74-75` from 3 attempts to 5-6 attempts, or increase interval from 1s to 2s
   - Gives backend more time to complete title generation before UI gives up
   - **Trade-off**: Slightly slower "New Chat" fallback on LLM failure, but more likely to catch successful generation

2. **Add retry mechanism to conversation-titles plugin**
   - Track failed title generations (conversation_id ŌåÆ failure reason)
   - On subsequent turns beyond the 2nd assistant turn, retry if the title is still NULL
   - Removes the hard "only try once" constraint (line 290)
   - **Requires schema change** to track retry state (or use a separate store)

3. **Surface storage/model resolution issues in logs**
   - Change `resolveTitleModel()` fallback logs from DEBUG to INFO/WARN
   - Helps operators debug why expected models aren't being used

### **Medium-term fixes (correctness):**

4. **Implement title refresh polling on the UI side**
   - After initial `generateTitle()` polling gives up, don't immediately show "New Chat"
   - Keep polling in background at lower frequency (every 5-10 seconds) until title arrives or user navigates
   - Show loading state instead of "New Chat" while waiting

5. **Make title generation idempotent with a retry budget**
   - Track assistant turn count where title was attempted
   - Allow re-attempts up to turn 3 or 4 (not just turn 1)
   - Document the intent: "We only generate once, by design, to avoid LLM cost explosion"

6. **Add fallback title generation from first user message**
   - If auto-title fails after turn 1, extract first N words from user's message as a fallback
   - Ensures conversation always has **some** title, even if LLM fails
   - Implement in `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:305-322`

---

## **EXACT ROOT CAUSE FOR THE BUG REPORT**

**"Chat session titles aren't being consistently generated for new chats"** is caused by:

1. **Primary**: Race condition where title generation takes >3 seconds, but UI polling gives up after 3 seconds ŌåÆ shows "New Chat" while backend still working
2. **Contributing**: LLM errors and validation rejections silently fail with no automatic retry; once first turn fails, there's no second chance
3. **Contributing**: No fallback when title generation fails; conversation remains untitled forever

The inconsistency occurs because:
- **Fast LLM responses** (< 3 seconds): Title generates successfully, shows on sidebar
- **Slow LLM responses** (> 3 seconds): UI polls timeout, shows "New Chat", but backend eventually generates real title (user sees inconsistency on refresh)
- **LLM errors**: Error logged, title stays NULL forever, sidebar shows empty/placeholder

---

## **KEY FILE REFERENCES**

| File | Key Lines | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts` | 254-342 | `handleTurnEnd()` ŌĆö the core title generation logic; checks `assistantTurnCount !== 1` at line 290 |
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts` | 189-252 | `resolveTitleModel()` ŌĆö fallback logic for storage, model resolution |
| `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts` | 26-43 | `validateGeneratedTitle()` ŌĆö rejects empty, "Untitled", whitespace |
| `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts` | 68-98 | `generateTitle()` ŌĆö UI polling with 3 attempts at 1-second intervals |
| `packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts` | 291-522 | `postMessage()` ŌĆö creates conversation, dispatches agent:invoke (fire-and-forget, line 515) |
| `packages/conversations/src/store.ts` | 610-629 | `setTitle()` ŌĆö implements `ifNull: true` compare-and-set logic |

Perfect! Now I can see clearly. The runner:
1. Receives the user message in the inbox as an `entry.payload` with both `content` (text) and `contentBlocks` (attachment blocks)
2. Translates the `contentBlocks` via `translateContentBlocks()` which converts `attachment` blocks to either `image`, `document`, or text blocks (the text-mention)
3. Sends this **translated** content to the SDK as `messageContent`
4. The SDK writes the translated content to the jsonl
5. **The runner NEVER writes the original `attachment` blocks to the jsonl** - only the translated versions go through

Then when loading from history:
1. `conversations:get` reads the jsonl via `parseJsonlToTurns()`
2. The jsonl only contains the translated text blocks with the "User attached..." text
3. The UI renders this as plain text instead of a chip

Now let me write up the complete findings:

## INVESTIGATION COMPLETE

Based on my thorough code investigation, I can **CONFIRM YOUR HYPOTHESIS WITH EXACT ROOT CAUSE** and provide the precise evidence.

### (a) HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMATION

**The hypothesis is CORRECT**: The original `attachment` ContentBlock is lost on history load because the runner's native jsonl transcript (.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl) contains the TRANSLATED content the SDK saw (text-mention), not the original attachment block.

### (b) EXACT ROOT CAUSE LOCATION

The attachment block is lost at this specific point:

**File**: `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts`  
**Lines**: 420-428

```typescript
const messageContent: unknown = hasBlocks
  ? [
      ...(userText.length > 0 ? [{ type: 'text', text: userText }] : []),
      ...(await translateContentBlocks(entry.payload.contentBlocks!, {
        readWorkspace: workspaceReader,
        supportsDocumentBlocks: SUPPORTS_DOCUMENT_BLOCKS,
      })),
    ]
  : userText;
```

This is where the **original `attachment` blocks from `entry.payload.contentBlocks` are irreversibly transformed**:

- Input: `attachment` ContentBlock with `{ path, displayName, mediaType, sizeBytes }`  
- Output: ONE OF:
  - `{ type: 'document', source: { type: 'base64', ... } }` (if PDF + SUPPORTS_DOCUMENT_BLOCKS)
  - `{ type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', ... } }` (if image bytes readable)
  - `{ type: 'text', text: '...' }` inlined content (if small text file)
  - **`{ type: 'text', text: "User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)" }`** ŌåÉ For PDF or unreadable files

The **critical problem**: The `messageContent` (the translated blocks) is what gets yielded to the SDK at line 449, and the SDK writes this to its native jsonl. The original `entry.payload.contentBlocks` is **never persisted** anywhere accessible at history-read time.

### (c) JSONL PRESERVATION CHECK

**Does the jsonl preserve attachment blocks or only translated text?**

**ANSWER: ONLY TRANSLATED TEXT**

Evidence chain:

1. **Attachment translation** (lines 420-428 in main.ts) - `attachment` blocks are translated immediately before SDK handoff
2. **SDK handoff** (line 449) - The SDK receives `messageContent` which contains the translated blocks, **not the original attachment blocks**
3. **JSONL write** - The Anthropic SDK writes `message.content` (which is `messageContent` now) to `.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl`
4. **JSONL parsing** (parse.ts line 150) - `normalizeContent()` extracts `message.content` from each jsonl line and validates blocks via `ContentBlockSchema.safeParse()`
5. **Text-mention in jsonl** - For a PDF that couldn't be read or doesn't support document blocks, the jsonl contains: `{ type: 'text', text: "User attached 'Disaster Recovery Plan - v3.pdf' at .ax/uploads/... (application/pdf)" }`

The text-mention string comes **directly from attachment-translation.ts line 87**:

```typescript
function textMention(att: AttachmentBlock): { type: 'text'; text: string } {
  return {
    type: 'text',
    text: `User attached '${att.displayName}' at ${att.path} (${att.mediaType})`,
  };
}
```

This matches the bug report's exact observed text.

### (d) CHANNEL-WEB RENDER PATH

When a stored message is loaded from history and rendered:

1. **GET /api/chat/conversations/:id** (routes-chat.ts:567-605) calls `conversations:get`
2. **conversations:get** (plugin.ts:662-718) reads the runner's jsonl via `readTranscriptFromWorkspace()` (lines 733-756)
3. **parseJsonlToTurns()** (parse.ts:109-196) extracts `message.content` from jsonl - which contains the translated text block, NOT the original attachment block
4. **history-adapter.ts:blocksToParts()** (lines 63-169) processes the ContentBlocks:
   - Line 69-71: `text` blocks ŌåÆ rendered as text parts
   - Line 107-119: `attachment` blocks ŌåÆ rendered as file parts with attachment chip
   - **BUT** the text-mention is a `text` block, so it renders as plain text (line 70)

The history-adapter can **only render attachment chips for `attachment` ContentBlocks** (line 107-119), but the jsonl contains text blocks, not attachment blocks.

### (e) RECOMMENDED FIX APPROACH

**Option 1: Store original attachment blocks separately (RECOMMENDED)**

- Store the original `entry.payload.contentBlocks` (attachment blocks) in a host-side table keyed by `(conversationId, turnId)`
- At history read time, look up and merge the original attachment blocks into the turn's contentBlocks
- Pros: Minimal changes, clean separation of concerns, attachment chip renders correctly
- Cons: Requires schema change

**Option 2: Reconstruct from text-mention (NOT RECOMMENDED)**

- Parse the "User attached '...' at <path> (<mime>)" text string on the read path
- Reconstruct `attachment` blocks from the parsed components
- Pros: No schema change
- Cons: Fragile (parsing user-facing strings), loses sizeBytes info, error-prone

**Option 3: Store serialized attachment blocks in a metadata field (ACCEPTABLE)**

- Store `JSON.stringify(originalAttachmentBlocks)` in a `metadata` column on the conversation_turns (if it still exists) or conversation_attachments table
- Parse on read
- Pros: Single schema change, preserves all info
- Cons: Requires careful validation

**Option 4: Bypass translation for user-sent attachments (ALTERNATIVE)**

- Only translate attachments when **forwarding to the SDK model**, not when persisting
- Store the original attachment blocks in the jsonl separately from what the SDK sees
- Cons: Requires changes to how the SDK receives and persists messages

## SUMMARY

| Finding | Answer |
|---------|--------|
| **Hypothesis correct?** | YES - confirmed |
| **Root cause file** | `/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:420-428` |
| **Root cause line** | Line 423 `translateContentBlocks()` transform is irreversible |
| **What gets written to jsonl** | Translated text blocks only (text-mention strings for PDFs) |
| **What render path receives** | Text blocks from jsonl parse, no attachment blocks to convert to chips |
| **Fix location** | Host-side storage of original attachment blocks keyed by (conversationId, turnId) |
| **Recommended fix** | Store original `ContentBlock[]` in database, merge at history-read time |

  - `{ type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', ... } }` (if image bytes readable)
  - `{ type: 'text', text: '...' }` inlined content (if small text file)
  - **`{ type: 'text', text: "User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)" }`** ŌåÉ For PDF or unreadable files

The **critical problem**: The `messageContent` (the translated blocks) is what gets yielded to the SDK at line 449, and the SDK writes this to its native jsonl. The original `entry.payload.contentBlocks` is **never persisted** anywhere accessible at history-read time.

### (c) JSONL PRESERVATION CHECK

**Does the jsonl preserve attachment blocks or only translated text?**

**ANSWER: ONLY TRANSLATED TEXT**

Evidence chain:

1. **Attachment translation** (lines 420-428 in main.ts) - `attachment` blocks are translated immediately before SDK handoff
2. **SDK handoff** (line 449) - The SDK receives `messageContent` which contains the translated blocks, **not the original attachment blocks**
3. **JSONL write** - The Anthropic SDK writes `message.content` (which is `messageContent` now) to `.claude/projects/<sessionId>.jsonl`
4. **JSONL parsing** (parse.ts line 150) - `normalizeContent()` extracts `message.content` from each jsonl line and validates blocks via `ContentBlockSchema.safeParse()`
5. **Text-mention in jsonl** - For a PDF that couldn't be read or doesn't support document blocks, the jsonl contains: `{ type: 'text', text: "User attached 'Disaster Recovery Plan - v3.pdf' at .ax/uploads/... (application/pdf)" }`

The text-mention string comes **directly from attachment-translation.ts line 87**:

```typescript
function textMention(att: AttachmentBlock): { type: 'text'; text: string } {
  return {
    type: 'text',
    text: `User attached '${att.displayName}' at ${att.path} (${att.mediaType})`,
  };
}
```

This matches the bug report's exact observed text.

### (d) CHANNEL-WEB RENDER PATH

When a stored message is loaded from history and rendered:

1. **GET /api/chat/conversations/:id** (routes-chat.ts:567-605) calls `conversations:get`
2. **conversations:get** (plugin.ts:662-718) reads the runner's jsonl via `readTranscriptFromWorkspace()` (lines 733-756)
3. **parseJsonlToTurns()** (parse.ts:109-196) extracts `message.content` from jsonl - which contains the translated text block, NOT the original attachment block
4. **history-adapter.ts:blocksToParts()** (lines 63-169) processes the ContentBlocks:
   - Line 69-71: `text` blocks ŌåÆ rendered as text parts
   - Line 107-119: `attachment` blocks ŌåÆ rendered as file parts with attachment chip
   - **BUT** the text-mention is a `text` block, so it renders as plain text (line 70)

The history-adapter can **only render attachment chips for `attachment` ContentBlocks** (line 107-119), but the jsonl contains text blocks, not attachment blocks.

### (e) RECOMMENDED FIX APPROACH

**Option 1: Store original attachment blocks separately (RECOMMENDED)**

- Store the original `entry.payload.contentBlocks` (attachment blocks) in a host-side table keyed by `(conversationId, turnId)`
- At history read time, look up and merge the original attachment blocks into the turn's contentBlocks
- Pros: Minimal changes, clean separation of concerns, attachment chip renders correctly
- Cons: Requires schema change

**Option 2: Reconstruct from text-mention (NOT RECOMMENDED)**

- Parse the "User attached '...' at <path> (<mime>)" text string on the read path
- Reconstruct `attachment` blocks from the parsed components
- Pros: No schema change
- Cons: Fragile (parsing user-facing strings), loses sizeBytes info, error-prone

**Option 3: Store serialized attachment blocks in a metadata field (ACCEPTABLE)**

- Store `JSON.stringify(originalAttachmentBlocks)` in a `metadata` column on the conversation_turns (if it still exists) or conversation_attachments table
- Parse on read
- Pros: Single schema change, preserves all info
- Cons: Requires careful validation

**Option 4: Bypass translation for user-sent attachments (ALTERNATIVE)**

- Only translate attachments when **forwarding to the SDK model**, not when persisting
- Store the original attachment blocks in the jsonl separately from what the SDK sees
- Cons: Requires changes to how the SDK receives and persists messages

## SUMMARY

| Finding | Answer |
|---------|--------|
| **Hypothesis correct?** | YES - confirmed |
| **Root cause file** | `/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:420-428` |
| **Root cause line** | Line 423 `translateContentBlocks()` transform is irreversible |
| **What gets written to jsonl** | Translated text blocks only (text-mention strings for PDFs) |
| **What render path receives** | Text blocks from jsonl parse, no attachment blocks to convert to chips |
| **Fix location** | Host-side storage of original attachment blocks keyed by (conversationId, turnId) |
| **Recommended fix** | Store original `ContentBlock[]` in database, merge at history-read time |

content.text: Both root causes are clear. Bug 1 has several contributing causes (the agent listed 7 ŌĆö I need to verify which actually matter). Bug 2 is confirmed: the runner translates attachment blocks ŌåÆ text-mention before the SDK writes the jsonl, so history-load (which reads the jsonl) only sees text. Before designing, I'll read the ground-truth files myself for the key decision points, and dispatch one focused Explore on whether the host already persists attachment metadata per conversation (which determines bug 2's fix shape).

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Investigation Report: Attachment Persistence in ax-next

### 1. HOST DATABASE SCHEMA ŌĆö ATTACHMENTS TABLES

The `@ax/attachments` plugin owns **only one table**, `attachments_v1_temps`, used for temporary staging. There is **NO host-side table for committed attachments**.

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/migrations.ts:1-61`

**`attachments_v1_temps` table schema (all columns):**
| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
|--------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| `attachment_id` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | PRIMARY KEY. Unique temp staging id. |
| `user_id` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | FK to requesting user. Indexed for quota queries. |
| `bytes` | BYTEA | NO | ŌĆö | File contents (max 25 MiB). Short-lived (TTL 10 min default). |
| `display_name` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | Original filename from uploader. |
| `media_type` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | MIME type (allowlist-validated). |
| `size_bytes` | BIGINT | NO | CHECK Ōēź 0 | Byte count. Used for per-user quota enforcement. |
| `expires_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | NO | ŌĆö | Expiration time. Indexed for janitor TTL sweep. |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | NO | NOW() | Row creation timestamp. |

**Indexes:**
- `attachments_v1_temps_user_id_idx` ŌĆö for per-user pending-bytes quota aggregation.
- `attachments_v1_temps_expires_at_idx` ŌĆö for TTL janitor's `WHERE expires_at <= now()` sweep.

**Critical: No committed attachments table.** Once `attachments:commit` runs, the file is written to the workspace (git-backed) and the temp row is deleted (line 240 in handlers.ts). Committed attachments exist **only in the workspace filesystem**, not in any host database table.

---

### 2. ATTACHMENTS:COMMIT SERVICE HOOK ŌĆö WHAT IT PERSISTS

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:153-256` (createCommitHandler)
**Called from:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:363-370`

**Input shape** (lines 193-197 in routes-chat.ts):
```typescript
interface AttachmentsCommitInput {
  attachmentId: string;
  conversationId: string;
  turnId: string;
}
```

**Process:**
1. **Retrieve temp row** (line 158): `store.getTemp(attachmentId)` ŌĆö fails with 'not-found' if absent/expired.
2. **User validation** (line 167): Verify `row.userId === ctx.userId` ŌĆö fails with 'forbidden' if foreign user.
3. **Build path** (line 179): 
   ```
   path = `.ax/uploads/${input.conversationId}/${input.turnId}/${filenameComponent}`
   ```
   Where `filenameComponent` = 8-hex-char prefix + `__` + sanitized filename (line 150).
4. **Compute SHA256** (line 180): Hash of file bytes.
5. **workspace:apply** (lines 197-206): Write bytes to workspace at the computed path. Retries up to 5 times on parent-mismatch.
6. **Delete temp row** (lines 240): Best-effort; janitor reaps leftovers on failure.
7. **Return output** (lines 248-254):
   ```typescript
   {
     path: string,           // e.g., ".ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/12ab34cd__foo.txt"
     sha256: string,         // Hex-encoded file hash
     mediaType: string,      // Preserved from temp row
     sizeBytes: number,      // Preserved from temp row
     displayName: string     // Preserved from temp row (NOT sanitized on-disk)
   }
   ```

**Critical facts:**
- The **committed record retains conversationId and turnId** in its path structure.
- The **turnId comes from the user-message handler**, not the attachment request.
- There is **no separate host-side row** ŌĆö persistence is **workspace filesystem only**.
- The temp row is **deleted after successful commit** ŌĆö no history in `attachments_v1_temps`.

---

### 3. UPLOAD LIFECYCLE: TEMP ŌåÆ COMMITTED PATH STRUCTURE

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:72-151` (createStoreTempHandler & sanitizeFilenameComponent)

**Temp storage phase** (`attachments:store-temp`):
- **Input:** `{ bytes, displayName, mediaType }`
- **Output:** `{ attachmentId, sizeBytes, expiresAt }` ŌĆö no path yet.
- **Storage:** Inserted into `attachments_v1_temps` with `attachment_id = randomUUID()`, expires in 10 min (default).

**Committed path structure** (line 179):
```
.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<filenameComponent>
```

**Segment decoding:**
- **`.ax/uploads/`** ŌĆö workspace-relative prefix (hardcoded namespace).
- **`<conversationId>`** ŌĆö e.g., `cnv_AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp` (from `mintConversationId()` in store.ts:143, format: `cnv_` + base64url(16 random bytes)).
- **`<turnId>`** ŌĆö e.g., `req-AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp` or UUID (from `makeReqId()` in routes-chat.ts:348, the **same id as the user-message's turnId**, not the requestId). Minted server-side at message post time (routes-chat.ts:348).
- **`<filenameComponent>`** ŌĆö format: `<8-hex>__<sanitized-name>` where:
  - `<8-hex>` = 8 random hex chars (4 bytes from `randomBytes(4).toString('hex')`) to prevent collisions within the same `(conversationId, turnId)`.
  - `<sanitized-name>` = original `display_name` with non-alphanumeric chars collapsed, leading dots stripped, runs of dots collapsed to single dot, preserving the extension.

**Example:** `.ax/uploads/cnv_AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp/req-XyZaBcDeF/a1b2c3d4__budget_2025.pdf`

---

### 4. HOST-SIDE LOOKUP: "ALL ATTACHMENT BLOCKS FOR CONVERSATION X"

**NO native host-side table** exists. Committed attachments are **NOT queryable at the database level** because they live in the workspace filesystem.

**Current feasible approach:**
1. **Via `conversations:get`** ŌåÆ retrieve conversation metadata + call `workspace:list`.
2. **Parse the jsonl transcript** (via `readTranscriptFromWorkspace`, plugin.ts:733-756) ŌåÆ extract all `attachment` ContentBlocks.
3. **Filter by path prefix** `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` to find committed attachments.

**Files:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:702-716` (getConversation calls readTranscriptFromWorkspace).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:733-756` (readTranscriptFromWorkspace ŌĆö globs `.claude/projects/**/<sessionId>.jsonl`).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host/src/parse.ts:109-196` (parseJsonlToTurns).

**No dedicated hook** like `attachments:list-for-conversation` exists. The integration is implicit: channel-web receives turns from `conversations:get`, renders ContentBlocks of type `attachment` (which carry the path in the block itself).

---

### 5. CHANNEL-WEB POST /api/chat/messages: ATTACHMENT_REF ŌåÆ ATTACHMENT

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:332-396`

**Flow:**

1. **Mint server-side identifiers** (line 348):
   ```typescript
   const userTurnId = makeReqId();  // UUIDv4, e.g., "req-AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp"
   ```
   This is the **turnId** passed to `attachments:commit` (line 369).

2. **Iterate attachment_ref blocks** (lines 357-396):
   ```typescript
   for (const block of body.contentBlocks) {
     if (block.type !== 'attachment_ref') { ... }
     const committed = await bus.call('attachments:commit', attachmentCtx, {
       attachmentId: block.attachmentId,
       conversationId,
       turnId: userTurnId,  // SAME turnId as the user message
     });
     // Rewrite attachment_ref ŌåÆ attachment ContentBlock
     rewrittenBlocks.push({
       type: 'attachment',
       path: committed.path,        // e.g., ".ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/abc123__foo.pdf"
       displayName: committed.displayName,
       mediaType: committed.mediaType,
       sizeBytes: committed.sizeBytes,
     });
   }
   ```

3. **Bind to message** (line 485-492):
   ```typescript
   const message: AgentMessage = {
     role: 'user',
     content: extractText(rewrittenBlocks),
     ...(nonTextBlocks.length > 0 ? { contentBlocks: nonTextBlocks } : {}),
     turnId: userTurnId,  // Passed to runner for jsonl binding
   };
   ```

**Identifiers tying committed attachment to user turn:**
- **`turnId`** (same `userTurnId` from line 348): embedded in the file path (e.g., `.ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/<turnId>/file`).
- **`conversationId`**: embedded in the file path (e.g., `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/...`).
- The path itself becomes the canonical reference in the `attachment` ContentBlock.

**The attachment path IS recoverable later** because:
- The runner writes the user turn to the jsonl with `turnId: userTurnId` (message.turnId field).
- The jsonl turn's contentBlocks include the `attachment` block with the `path` field.
- When `conversations:get` reads the jsonl, it parses this path from the ContentBlock (ipc-protocol schema).

---

### 6. PARSED JSONL TURNS: AVAILABLE IDENTIFIERS

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host/src/parse.ts:44-50`

**ParsedTurn interface returned by parseJsonlToTurns:**
```typescript
export interface ParsedTurn {
  turnId: string;          // SDK's uuid field ŌĆö matches message.turnId from AgentMessage
  turnIndex: number;       // Sequential index (0, 1, 2, ...)
  role: ParsedTurnRole;    // 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool'
  contentBlocks: ContentBlock[];  // Includes attachment blocks with path
  createdAt: string;       // ISO-8601 timestamp
}
```

**Attachment matching capability:**

The parsed turn **carries `turnId`** (line 179 in parse.ts):
```typescript
turns.push({
  turnId: uuid,  // From SDK jsonl envelope
  ...
});
```

This `turnId` (which is the **SDK's `uuid` field**, not a separate request id) **matches the channel-web-minted `userTurnId`** because:
1. Channel-web mints `userTurnId = makeReqId()` (routes-chat.ts:348).
2. Channel-web passes `turnId: userTurnId` to `agent:invoke` via `AgentMessage.turnId` (routes-chat.ts:492).
3. The runner's first SDK turn captures this in `message.turnId` field and writes `uuid: <the-sdk's-uuid>` to the jsonl.
4. Parse.ts extracts `uuid` as the parsed `turnId`.

**YES: The parsed Turn carries an id (turnId) matchable to attachment records** ŌĆö but only if the runner's SDK emissions include the message.turnId that channel-web supplied. The jsonl line is the source of truth; the channel-web commitment path is reconstructed from `conversationId + turnId + filename` on the fly during commit.

---

### SUMMARY TABLE

| Question | Answer | Location |
|----------|--------|----------|
| **(a) Full schema of attachments table(s)** | Single table: `attachments_v1_temps` with 8 columns (attachment_id PK, user_id, bytes BYTEA, display_name, media_type, size_bytes, expires_at, created_at). Two indexes on user_id and expires_at. **NO committed attachments table.** | migrations.ts:1-61 |
| **(b) Committed attachments retain conversationId + per-turn identifier?** | YES ŌĆö path format `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<filename>` embeds both. TurnId = server-minted UUID tied to the user message. | handlers.ts:179, routes-chat.ts:348-369 |
| **(c) Exact upload path structure** | `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<8-hex-rand>__<sanitized-displayName>` where conversationId = `cnv_<base64url>`, turnId = server UUID, sanitized = collision-prefix + filename with unsafe chars collapsed. | handlers.ts:142-151, 179 |
| **(d) Host-side lookup of "all attachments for conversation X" feasible?** | Not directly at DB level (no table). **Feasible via transcript parsing:** `conversations:get` ŌåÆ `parseJsonlToTurns` ŌåÆ filter ContentBlocks where type='attachment'. No dedicated hook; integration is implicit via transcript parsing. | plugin.ts:702-756, parse.ts:109-196 |
| **(e) Parsed jsonl turns expose id matchable to attachment records?** | YES ŌĆö `ParsedTurn.turnId` (SDK's uuid field) matches channel-web's `userTurnId` if runner preserves message.turnId in jsonl. The attachment path includes this turnId, so turns can reconstruct the lookup. | parse.ts:44-50, 179 |

---

### THE BUG: ATTACHMENT CHIPS NOT RENDERING ON HISTORY LOAD

**Likely root cause:** When `conversations:get` reads the jsonl and parses turns into ContentBlocks, the `attachment` blocks have a `path` field (e.g., `.ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/abc123__file.pdf`), but:

1. **The path may be constructed at commit time with a turnId that doesn't match** what the jsonl turn's uuid field is (if channel-web's userTurnId isn't properly threaded through to the runner).
2. **The attachment block may be missing from the parsed transcript** if the jsonl parsing (ipc-protocol schema validation) rejects the block (untrusted content, I5).
3. **The workspace:list glob** (plugin.ts:741) may fail to find the jsonl file if runnerSessionId is null or the workspace lookup uses the wrong context.
4. **The channel-web frontend may fail to fetch the attachment** if the path is malformed or the `attachments:download` handler's path-scope check (handlers.ts:301-347) rejects it (path not under `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` or not found in transcript blocks).

To debug: Verify that (1) channel-web's userTurnId matches the runner's jsonl uuid; (2) the jsonl contentBlocks carry valid attachment blocks; (3) the path construction is deterministic across commit + parse + download.

## Investigation Report: Attachment Persistence in ax-next

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/migrations.ts:1-61`

**`attachments_v1_temps` table schema (all columns):**
| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
|--------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| `attachment_id` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | PRIMARY KEY. Unique temp staging id. |
| `user_id` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | FK to requesting user. Indexed for quota queries. |
| `bytes` | BYTEA | NO | ŌĆö | File contents (max 25 MiB). Short-lived (TTL 10 min default). |
| `display_name` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | Original filename from uploader. |
| `media_type` | TEXT | NO | ŌĆö | MIME type (allowlist-validated). |
| `size_bytes` | BIGINT | NO | CHECK Ōēź 0 | Byte count. Used for per-user quota enforcement. |
| `expires_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | NO | ŌĆö | Expiration time. Indexed for janitor TTL sweep. |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ | NO | NOW() | Row creation timestamp. |

**Indexes:**
- `attachments_v1_temps_user_id_idx` ŌĆö for per-user pending-bytes quota aggregation.
- `attachments_v1_temps_expires_at_idx` ŌĆö for TTL janitor's `WHERE expires_at <= now()` sweep.

**Critical: No committed attachments table.** Once `attachments:commit` runs, the file is written to the workspace (git-backed) and the temp row is deleted (line 240 in handlers.ts). Committed attachments exist **only in the workspace filesystem**, not in any host database table.

---

### 2. ATTACHMENTS:COMMIT SERVICE HOOK ŌĆö WHAT IT PERSISTS

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:153-256` (createCommitHandler)
**Called from:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:363-370`

**Input shape** (lines 193-197 in routes-chat.ts):
```typescript
interface AttachmentsCommitInput {
  attachmentId: string;
  conversationId: string;
  turnId: string;
}
```

**Process:**
1. **Retrieve temp row** (line 158): `store.getTemp(attachmentId)` ŌĆö fails with 'not-found' if absent/expired.
2. **User validation** (line 167): Verify `row.userId === ctx.userId` ŌĆö fails with 'forbidden' if foreign user.
3. **Build path** (line 179): 
   ```
   path = `.ax/uploads/${input.conversationId}/${input.turnId}/${filenameComponent}`
   ```
   Where `filenameComponent` = 8-hex-char prefix + `__` + sanitized filename (line 150).
4. **Compute SHA256** (line 180): Hash of file bytes.
5. **workspace:apply** (lines 197-206): Write bytes to workspace at the computed path. Retries up to 5 times on parent-mismatch.
6. **Delete temp row** (lines 240): Best-effort; janitor reaps leftovers on failure.
7. **Return output** (lines 248-254):
   ```typescript
   {
     path: string,           // e.g., ".ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/12ab34cd__foo.txt"
     sha256: string,         // Hex-encoded file hash
     mediaType: string,      // Preserved from temp row
     sizeBytes: number,      // Preserved from temp row
     displayName: string     // Preserved from temp row (NOT sanitized on-disk)
   }
   ```

**Critical facts:**
- The **committed record retains conversationId and turnId** in its path structure.
- The **turnId comes from the user-message handler**, not the attachment request.
- There is **no separate host-side row** ŌĆö persistence is **workspace filesystem only**.
- The temp row is **deleted after successful commit** ŌĆö no history in `attachments_v1_temps`.

---

### 3. UPLOAD LIFECYCLE: TEMP ŌåÆ COMMITTED PATH STRUCTURE

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/attachments/src/handlers.ts:72-151` (createStoreTempHandler & sanitizeFilenameComponent)

**Temp storage phase** (`attachments:store-temp`):
- **Input:** `{ bytes, displayName, mediaType }`
- **Output:** `{ attachmentId, sizeBytes, expiresAt }` ŌĆö no path yet.
- **Storage:** Inserted into `attachments_v1_temps` with `attachment_id = randomUUID()`, expires in 10 min (default).

**Committed path structure** (line 179):
```
.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<filenameComponent>
```

**Segment decoding:**
- **`.ax/uploads/`** ŌĆö workspace-relative prefix (hardcoded namespace).
- **`<conversationId>`** ŌĆö e.g., `cnv_AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp` (from `mintConversationId()` in store.ts:143, format: `cnv_` + base64url(16 random bytes)).
- **`<turnId>`** ŌĆö e.g., `req-AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp` or UUID (from `makeReqId()` in routes-chat.ts:348, the **same id as the user-message's turnId**, not the requestId). Minted server-side at message post time (routes-chat.ts:348).
- **`<filenameComponent>`** ŌĆö format: `<8-hex>__<sanitized-name>` where:
  - `<8-hex>` = 8 random hex chars (4 bytes from `randomBytes(4).toString('hex')`) to prevent collisions within the same `(conversationId, turnId)`.
  - `<sanitized-name>` = original `display_name` with non-alphanumeric chars collapsed, leading dots stripped, runs of dots collapsed to single dot, preserving the extension.

**Example:** `.ax/uploads/cnv_AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp/req-XyZaBcDeF/a1b2c3d4__budget_2025.pdf`

---

### 4. HOST-SIDE LOOKUP: "ALL ATTACHMENT BLOCKS FOR CONVERSATION X"

**NO native host-side table** exists. Committed attachments are **NOT queryable at the database level** because they live in the workspace filesystem.

**Current feasible approach:**
1. **Via `conversations:get`** ŌåÆ retrieve conversation metadata + call `workspace:list`.
2. **Parse the jsonl transcript** (via `readTranscriptFromWorkspace`, plugin.ts:733-756) ŌåÆ extract all `attachment` ContentBlocks.
3. **Filter by path prefix** `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` to find committed attachments.

**Files:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:702-716` (getConversation calls readTranscriptFromWorkspace).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts:733-756` (readTranscriptFromWorkspace ŌĆö globs `.claude/projects/**/<sessionId>.jsonl`).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host/src/parse.ts:109-196` (parseJsonlToTurns).

**No dedicated hook** like `attachments:list-for-conversation` exists. The integration is implicit: channel-web receives turns from `conversations:get`, renders ContentBlocks of type `attachment` (which carry the path in the block itself).

---

### 5. CHANNEL-WEB POST /api/chat/messages: ATTACHMENT_REF ŌåÆ ATTACHMENT

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts:332-396`

**Flow:**

1. **Mint server-side identifiers** (line 348):
   ```typescript
   const userTurnId = makeReqId();  // UUIDv4, e.g., "req-AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOp"
   ```
   This is the **turnId** passed to `attachments:commit` (line 369).

2. **Iterate attachment_ref blocks** (lines 357-396):
   ```typescript
   for (const block of body.contentBlocks) {
     if (block.type !== 'attachment_ref') { ... }
     const committed = await bus.call('attachments:commit', attachmentCtx, {
       attachmentId: block.attachmentId,
       conversationId,
       turnId: userTurnId,  // SAME turnId as the user message
     });
     // Rewrite attachment_ref ŌåÆ attachment ContentBlock
     rewrittenBlocks.push({
       type: 'attachment',
       path: committed.path,        // e.g., ".ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/abc123__foo.pdf"
       displayName: committed.displayName,
       mediaType: committed.mediaType,
       sizeBytes: committed.sizeBytes,
     });
   }
   ```

3. **Bind to message** (line 485-492):
   ```typescript
   const message: AgentMessage = {
     role: 'user',
     content: extractText(rewrittenBlocks),
     ...(nonTextBlocks.length > 0 ? { contentBlocks: nonTextBlocks } : {}),
     turnId: userTurnId,  // Passed to runner for jsonl binding
   };
   ```

**Identifiers tying committed attachment to user turn:**
- **`turnId`** (same `userTurnId` from line 348): embedded in the file path (e.g., `.ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/<turnId>/file`).
- **`conversationId`**: embedded in the file path (e.g., `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/...`).
- The path itself becomes the canonical reference in the `attachment` ContentBlock.

**The attachment path IS recoverable later** because:
- The runner writes the user turn to the jsonl with `turnId: userTurnId` (message.turnId field).
- The jsonl turn's contentBlocks include the `attachment` block with the `path` field.
- When `conversations:get` reads the jsonl, it parses this path from the ContentBlock (ipc-protocol schema).

---

### 6. PARSED JSONL TURNS: AVAILABLE IDENTIFIERS

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host/src/parse.ts:44-50`

**ParsedTurn interface returned by parseJsonlToTurns:**
```typescript
export interface ParsedTurn {
  turnId: string;          // SDK's uuid field ŌĆö matches message.turnId from AgentMessage
  turnIndex: number;       // Sequential index (0, 1, 2, ...)
  role: ParsedTurnRole;    // 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool'
  contentBlocks: ContentBlock[];  // Includes attachment blocks with path
  createdAt: string;       // ISO-8601 timestamp
}
```

**Attachment matching capability:**

The parsed turn **carries `turnId`** (line 179 in parse.ts):
```typescript
turns.push({
  turnId: uuid,  // From SDK jsonl envelope
  ...
});
```

This `turnId` (which is the **SDK's `uuid` field**, not a separate request id) **matches the channel-web-minted `userTurnId`** because:
1. Channel-web mints `userTurnId = makeReqId()` (routes-chat.ts:348).
2. Channel-web passes `turnId: userTurnId` to `agent:invoke` via `AgentMessage.turnId` (routes-chat.ts:492).
3. The runner's first SDK turn captures this in `message.turnId` field and writes `uuid: <the-sdk's-uuid>` to the jsonl.
4. Parse.ts extracts `uuid` as the parsed `turnId`.

**YES: The parsed Turn carries an id (turnId) matchable to attachment records** ŌĆö but only if the runner's SDK emissions include the message.turnId that channel-web supplied. The jsonl line is the source of truth; the channel-web commitment path is reconstructed from `conversationId + turnId + filename` on the fly during commit.

---

### SUMMARY TABLE

| Question | Answer | Location |
|----------|--------|----------|
| **(a) Full schema of attachments table(s)** | Single table: `attachments_v1_temps` with 8 columns (attachment_id PK, user_id, bytes BYTEA, display_name, media_type, size_bytes, expires_at, created_at). Two indexes on user_id and expires_at. **NO committed attachments table.** | migrations.ts:1-61 |
| **(b) Committed attachments retain conversationId + per-turn identifier?** | YES ŌĆö path format `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<filename>` embeds both. TurnId = server-minted UUID tied to the user message. | handlers.ts:179, routes-chat.ts:348-369 |
| **(c) Exact upload path structure** | `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<8-hex-rand>__<sanitized-displayName>` where conversationId = `cnv_<base64url>`, turnId = server UUID, sanitized = collision-prefix + filename with unsafe chars collapsed. | handlers.ts:142-151, 179 |
| **(d) Host-side lookup of "all attachments for conversation X" feasible?** | Not directly at DB level (no table). **Feasible via transcript parsing:** `conversations:get` ŌåÆ `parseJsonlToTurns` ŌåÆ filter ContentBlocks where type='attachment'. No dedicated hook; integration is implicit via transcript parsing. | plugin.ts:702-756, parse.ts:109-196 |
| **(e) Parsed jsonl turns expose id matchable to attachment records?** | YES ŌĆö `ParsedTurn.turnId` (SDK's uuid field) matches channel-web's `userTurnId` if runner preserves message.turnId in jsonl. The attachment path includes this turnId, so turns can reconstruct the lookup. | parse.ts:44-50, 179 |

---

### THE BUG: ATTACHMENT CHIPS NOT RENDERING ON HISTORY LOAD

**Likely root cause:** When `conversations:get` reads the jsonl and parses turns into ContentBlocks, the `attachment` blocks have a `path` field (e.g., `.ax/uploads/cnv_xxx/req-yyy/abc123__file.pdf`), but:

1. **The path may be constructed at commit time with a turnId that doesn't match** what the jsonl turn's uuid field is (if channel-web's userTurnId isn't properly threaded through to the runner).
2. **The attachment block may be missing from the parsed transcript** if the jsonl parsing (ipc-protocol schema validation) rejects the block (untrusted content, I5).
3. **The workspace:list glob** (plugin.ts:741) may fail to find the jsonl file if runnerSessionId is null or the workspace lookup uses the wrong context.
4. **The channel-web frontend may fail to fetch the attachment** if the path is malformed or the `attachments:download` handler's path-scope check (handlers.ts:301-347) rejects it (path not under `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` or not found in transcript blocks).

To debug: Verify that (1) channel-web's userTurnId matches the runner's jsonl uuid; (2) the jsonl contentBlocks carry valid attachment blocks; (3) the path construction is deterministic across commit + parse + download.

lastPrompt: /yolo-ship fix all bugs in the "UI Bugs" section of TODO.md

content.text:
The attachments investigation is decisive: committed attachments live **only in the workspace filesystem** (path `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/<file>`), there's no host DB record, and the runner translates the attachment block ŌåÆ text-mention before the SDK writes the jsonl. So on history read the original block is genuinely gone ŌĆö only the model-facing translation survives.

The conceptual core: the jsonl is the *model's* view, but it's being used as the source of truth for the *user's* view, and those legitimately differ for attachments. Let me read the ground-truth files to finalize the fix design for both bugs.

lastPrompt: /yolo-ship fix all bugs in the "UI Bugs" section of TODO.md

content.text: The title plugin is clear. The killer line is `if (assistantTurnCount !== 1) return;` (plugin.ts:290) ŌĆö title generation is strictly one-shot on the first assistant turn. If that single attempt is skipped or fails for *any* transient reason (jsonl not yet readable when `conversations:get` runs, LLM error, validation rejection), the chat stays untitled forever. Combined with the `ifNull: true` guard already on set-title, relaxing this to a bounded retry is safe. Let me read the UI display path and bug-2's render/parse paths to finalize both designs.

UI side confirms a second contributing cause for the title bug: `generateTitle` (thread-list-adapter.ts:74) polls only 3├Ś1s then permanently caches `"New Chat"` in assistant-ui ŌĆö so even when the backend title lands a moment later, the sidebar shows "New Chat" until a full reload. So the title bug is two-headed: backend one-shot gate + UI poll giving up too early.

Now let me read bug-2's render and parse paths to finalize its fix shape.

lastPrompt: /yolo-ship fix all bugs in the "UI Bugs" section of TODO.md

content.text:
The render path confirms the mechanism precisely: `blocksToParts` already renders an `attachment` block as a chip (history-adapter.ts:107-119) ŌĆö the bug is purely that the jsonl yields a `text` block (the text-mention) instead of an `attachment` block (line 69-71 renders it as raw text). So the fix is to reconstruct the `attachment` block on the read path.

The text-mention embeds the full path (which itself encodes `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/`), displayName, and mediaType ŌĆö everything an `attachment` block needs except `sizeBytes`. Let me read the exact mention format, the ContentBlock schema, the jsonl parse shape, the postMessage rewrite, and what AttachmentChip renders, to finalize.

lastPrompt: /yolo-ship fix all bugs in the "UI Bugs" section of TODO.md

content.text: The text-mention format is exact: `` `User attached '${att.displayName}' at ${att.path} (${att.mediaType})` `` (attachment-translation.ts:87). The path embeds `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/<turnId>/`. Reconstructing an `attachment` block from this ŌĆö gated on the conversation's own upload prefix ŌĆö is the minimal correct fix, with the format owned by a shared helper to avoid drift. Let me read the schema, the parse shape, the read handler, and the chip to finalize sizeBytes handling.

755	  return parseJsonlToTurns(read.bytes);
756	}
