95	/**
96	 * Phase 1 (attachments & artifacts, 2026-05-15). Transient reference to a
97	 * pending upload staged in `@ax/attachments`'s temp store.
98	 *
99	 * Lives only on the POST /api/chat/messages request body. The chat-messages
100	 * handler resolves `attachmentId` ŌåÆ workspace path via `attachments:commit`
101	 * and rewrites this block as an `attachment` block BEFORE the message reaches
102	 * conversation storage or any subscriber. Never appears in stored transcripts.
103	 *
104	 * Boundary review (I1): `attachmentId` is workspace-vocab ŌĆö opaque server-
105	 * minted identifier, no backend leak (no `lfs_oid`, no `bucket`).
106	 */
107	export const AttachmentRefBlockSchema = z.object({
108	  type: z.literal('attachment_ref'),
109	  attachmentId: z.string().min(1),
110	});
111	export type AttachmentRefBlock = z.infer<typeof AttachmentRefBlockSchema>;
112	
113	/**
114	 * Phase 1 (attachments & artifacts, 2026-05-15). User-attached file OR
115	 * agent-published artifact as it appears in a stored conversation turn.
116	 *
117	 * The runner translates this variant to Anthropic-compatible types before
118	 * the LLM call (image/* ŌåÆ `image` block; PDF ŌåÆ `document` if SDK supports;
119	 * else text mention). This block is the canonical stored form (I4 ŌĆö single
120	 * source of truth); the Anthropic shape is derived per LLM call.
121	 *
122	 * `path` is workspace-relative (e.g. ".ax/uploads/<conv>/<turn>/file.pdf"),
123	 * not sandbox-absolute. Resolution: workspace:read(path) at current HEAD.
124	 */
125	/**
126	 * `path` is workspace-relative ŌĆö defense-in-depth refusal of absolute
127	 * paths, `..` traversal segments, Windows drive roots, and NUL bytes at
128	 * the wire boundary so a malformed value can't reach storage or
129	 * `attachments:download` and bypass the path-scope ACL.
130	 */
131	export function isWorkspaceRelativePath(value: string): boolean {
132	  if (value.startsWith('/') || value.startsWith('\\')) return false;
133	  if (value.includes('\0')) return false;
134	  if (/^[A-Za-z]:[\\/]/.test(value)) return false;
135	  for (const seg of value.split(/[\\/]/)) {
136	    if (seg === '..') return false;
137	  }
138	  return true;
139	}
140	
141	export const AttachmentBlockSchema = z.object({
142	  type: z.literal('attachment'),
143	  path: z
144	    .string()
145	    .min(1)
146	    .refine(
147	      isWorkspaceRelativePath,
148	      'path must be workspace-relative (no leading slash, no "..", no drive root, no NUL)',
149	    ),
150	  displayName: z.string().min(1),
151	  mediaType: z.string().min(1),
152	  sizeBytes: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
153	});
154	export type AttachmentBlock = z.infer<typeof AttachmentBlockSchema>;
155	
156	export const ContentBlockSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
157	  TextBlockSchema,
158	  ThinkingBlockSchema,
159	  RedactedThinkingBlockSchema,
160	  ToolUseBlockSchema,
161	  ToolResultBlockSchema,
162	  ImageBlockSchema,
163	  AttachmentRefBlockSchema,
164	  AttachmentBlockSchema,
165	]);
166	export type ContentBlock = z.infer<typeof ContentBlockSchema>;
167

content.text:
Now I have everything needed to finalize both designs. Key findings:

- **AttachmentChip** treats `sizeBytes` as optional and the history render path (`blocksToParts`) doesn't even pass it through ŌĆö so reconstructing an `attachment` block with `sizeBytes: 0` renders an identical chip. No fidelity loss for display.
- **parse.ts** validates every block via `ContentBlockSchema` and only `type:'user'`/`'assistant'` lines become turns (tool_result ŌåÆ role `'tool'`). The user turn's text-mention arrives as a `text` block. Reconstruction is safe and retroactively fixes already-stored chats (no migration).
- **content-blocks.ts:117-120** comment claiming the `attachment` block is "the canonical stored form" is now stale under runner-owned-sessions ŌĆö the jsonl stores the translation, which IS the bug.

Let me record decisions and check a few test/dependency facts before implementing.

content.content:
1	# Decisions
2	
3	Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted ŌĆö strikethrough if reversed.
4	
5	| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
6	|---|---|---|---|
7	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
8	| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
9	| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant ŌĆö any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
10	| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape ŌĆö internally inconsistent. Picked the conservative reading: ship the seam (`credentials:store-blob:*`) without churning all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 reshapes when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` actually needs `kind`. | Reshape now per Section 3 (rejected: ~600 LOC of churn across credential-proxy + mcp-client + CLI for no Phase 1b benefit) |
11	| 2026-04-28 | `@ax/credentials-store-db` (Phase 1b) delegates to `storage:get` / `storage:set` rather than owning its own table + migrations | Design Section 3 says "owns the credentials table + Kysely migrations, dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" ŌĆö but `@ax/database-sqlite` doesn't exist (only postgres has the hook), and the dedicated table earns its weight only with Phase 3 columns (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata`). The seam is still real: vault / KMS backends register `credentials:store-blob:*` against a different store. | Build `@ax/database-sqlite` first (rejected: bigger than Phase 1b); have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (rejected: deferred ŌĆö wait until Phase 3 schema needs it) |
12	| 2026-04-29 | Phase A spike confirms D1 (HOME-redirect): runner-owned-sessions design moves forward unchanged | Set HOME=tempdir, ran `query()` one-shot then `query({resume})` ŌĆö sessionId reattached, magic phrase recalled verbatim. Resume costs ~10├Ś less than turn 1 (validates "replay is wasteful"). Two follow-ups for Phase C: (1) workspace persistence ignore-list keeps only `.claude/projects/`, drops `.claude.json` + backups + policy-limits; (2) `runner:read-transcript` uses sessionId glob, not encoded-cwd reconstruction (SDK uses `realpath(cwd)`, breaks on macOS `/var` ŌåÆ `/private/var`). | Bind-mount `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>` to a workspace path (rejected: more sandbox-specific plumbing, doesn't generalize to k8s ŌĆö see design D1) |
13	| 2026-04-29 | No v1ŌåÆv2 schema migrations in ax-next, ever | ax-next is greenfield with no production data to preserve. The "forward-only via future v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" rule in `conversations_v1` was inherited from a different mental model. ALTER TABLE in place is correct now AND for every future schema change. The `_v1` suffix on existing tables (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`, etc.) is now just a stable identifier, not a version pointer. Confirmed by Vinay 2026-04-29 during Phase B planning. | Maintain v1 ŌåÆ v2 split for breaking changes (rejected: greenfield, no rows to migrate; speculative versioning earns nothing) |
14	| 2026-05-21 | Finding 3 facade lives in `@ax/core` (`registerWorkspaceApplyFacade`); backends rename their raw hook to `workspace:apply-internal`; callers of `workspace:apply` are untouched | Facade logic (fire pre-apply ŌåÆ call internal ŌåÆ fire applied) is backend-agnostic, so core is the principled single home (a future GCS backend reuses it). Renaming the *registration* (not the call) means every existing `call('workspace:apply')` site routes through the facade with zero churn ŌĆö the cleanest way to make the policy chokepoint unbypassable. `filterToPolicy` relocates to core (only depends on the `FileChange` core type) so the facade can policy-filter pre-apply identically to commit-notify. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-finding3-workspace-apply-facade.md`. | Per-backend duplicated facade (rejected: violates one-source-of-truth); facade in `@ax/workspace-git-core` (rejected: not backend-agnostic ŌĆö GCS won't import it); also route `apply-bundle`/commit-notify through the facade (rejected: commit-notify already fires both hooks, not a bypasser; out of finding-3 scope) |
15	| 2026-05-21 | New `yolo-ship` skill is an **orchestrator that sequences existing skills**, not a re-implementation | The brainstorm/design/implement/ship phases map 1:1 onto superpowers:brainstorming / writing-plans / subagent-driven-development / finishing-a-development-branch + commit-push-pr. The skill adds only the autonomy contract + the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop, and cross-references the rest by name (no @ links, per writing-skills). | Write a self-contained monolith re-explaining each phase (rejected: duplicates skills that already exist + evolve independently; bloats context) |
16	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` keeps context low via **subagent-driven implementation + Explore for reads**; orchestrator holds only plan + memory + summaries | User's hard cap is < 300ŌĆō400K tokens across all three phases. Per-task implementation subagents give ~50ŌĆō100├Ś savings; codebase exploration goes through Explore so file dumps never hit the orchestrator. | One long-running orchestrator agent doing everything inline (rejected: just relocates the context blowup); fully delegate brainstorm+design to a subagent too (rejected: orchestrator's judgment + memory are most valuable in those phases) |
17	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` runs brainstorming in **self-answering mode** and logs decisions instead of asking the user | User instruction: "don't ask questions unless absolutely necessary ŌĆö trust your recommendations as long as you document them in memory." Brainstorming is normally interactive; here the orchestrator generates the questions, answers from code/memory/conventions, and records each in decisions.md. AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
18	| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks goodŌĆ” you are done") ŌĆö no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |

input.command:
cd /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/fix-ui-bugs
cat >> .claude/memory/decisions.md << 'EOF'
| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300ŌĆō400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3├Ś1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload ŌĆö jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block ŌĆö so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
EOF
echo "decisions appended"

input.content:
# UI Bugs fix ŌĆö 2026-05-21

Closes the two items under `TODO.md` ŌåÆ "UI Bugs":

1. **Chat session titles aren't consistently generated for new chats.**
2. **Attachment chips don't render on history load** ŌĆö a PDF attachment shows
   the raw runner text-mention (`User attached 'ŌĆ”' at .ax/uploads/ŌĆ” (application/pdf)`)
   instead of a chip when the chat is reopened from the sidebar.

Root-cause analysis lives in `.claude/memory/decisions.md` (2026-05-21 rows).

---

## Bug 1 ŌĆö title generation

**Two independent causes, two fixes.**

### Cause A (backend, primary): one-shot title gate

`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts:290` skips unless
`assistantTurnCount === 1`. The title is attempted exactly once, at the first
assistant turn. If that single attempt is skipped or fails for *any* transient
reason ŌĆö `conversations:get` reading the runner jsonl before it's flushed,
`llm:call` error, validation rejecting the output ŌĆö the chat stays untitled
forever.

**Fix:** replace the strict gate with a bounded retry. Generate whenever the
conversation is still untitled (existing `title !== null` guard) and we're
within the first `MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS` (=3) assistant turns. `ifNull: true`
on `conversations:set-title` already makes re-attempts idempotent; the cap
bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable output.

- Task 1a: add `MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, swap the gate, update the comment.
- Task 1b (test): rewrite plugin.test.ts "more than one assistant turn" test
  (it encodes the old one-shot intent) to assert **retry while untitled**; add
  an **upper-bound** test (assistantTurnCount > MAX ŌåÆ skip).

### Cause B (UI, secondary): poll gives up too early

`packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts:74` polls 3├Ś1s then caches
`"New Chat"` in assistant-ui ŌĆö so even when the backend title lands a moment
later it shows "New Chat" until a full reload.

**Fix:** extract `pollConversationTitle(remoteId, opts)` with an injectable
`fetchImpl`/`baseDelayMs` seam; widen to ~10 attempts (early-returns on found).
`generateTitle` wraps it, falling back to "New Chat".

- Task 1c: extract + widen.
- Task 1d (test): add `pollConversationTitle` tests (resolves on a later poll;
  falls back to null after exhausting attempts).

Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a `list()`
refresh) ŌåÆ TODO.md.

---

## Bug 2 ŌĆö attachment chip on history load

Under runner-owned-sessions, `conversations:get` reads the runner's native jsonl
(`.claude/projects/<sid>.jsonl`). The runner translates the `attachment` block
to a model-facing **text-mention** *before* the SDK writes the jsonl
(`attachment-translation.ts:84-89`), so the original `attachment` block is gone ŌĆö
only the text survives. `history-adapter.blocksToParts` renders that `text`
block verbatim instead of the chip it would render for an `attachment` block.

**Fix:** reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the
host read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/`
prefix. The mention embeds path + displayName + mediaType (everything the chip
needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway ŌåÆ reconstruct with `0`).
No schema/table change; **retroactively fixes already-stored chats** (no migration).

- Task 2a: add `formatAttachmentMention` + `parseAttachmentMention` to
  `@ax/ipc-protocol/content-blocks.ts` (single source of truth for the format),
  plus round-trip + negative tests in `content-blocks.test.ts`. Update the stale
  "canonical stored form" comment.
- Task 2b: `attachment-translation.ts` `textMention` uses `formatAttachmentMention`
  (no behavior change).
- Task 2c: `conversations` `getConversation` post-processes user turns ŌĆö
  for each `role:'user'` turn, scan `text` blocks line-by-line; any line that
  `parseAttachmentMention` recognizes AND whose path starts with
  `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` AND that round-trips `AttachmentBlockSchema`
  becomes a reconstructed `attachment` block (preserving surrounding text + order).
- Task 2d (test): add reconstruction tests to `get-from-workspace.test.ts`
  (PDF text-mention ŌåÆ chip block; separate-block and merged-block cases;
  wrong-conversation-prefix path is NOT converted ŌĆö false-positive guard;
  assistant turns untouched).

### Security (untrusted content)

The jsonl is untrusted (model/SDK output; parse.ts already validates each block).
Reconstruction only touches `role:'user'` turns (model writes assistant/tool
lines, not user lines), gates the path to the conversation's own upload prefix,
and validates via `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` (rejects `..`, leading slash,
drive root, NUL). A crafted mention therefore can't yield a downloadable ref
outside the caller's own conversation. Run `security-checklist` before finishing.

### Boundary review

No hook signature changes. `conversations:get` wire shape is unchanged
(`{ conversation, turns }`); turns now carry the *original intended* `attachment`
block shape that the content-blocks comment already documents as canonical. The
two new functions are pure helpers on a shared library, not a hook surface.

---

## Gate

`pnpm build` + `pnpm test` (touched packages) + `pnpm lint`, then whole-branch
build. Filters: `@ax/ipc-protocol`, `@ax/conversation-titles`,
`@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/conversations`, `@ax/channel-web`.

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	
3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
4	// ContentBlock ŌĆö the canonical Anthropic-compatible content-block shape.
5	//
6	// This schema is the single source of truth for content blocks across:
7	//   - the IPC wire (runner ŌåÆ host event.turn-end)
8	//   - storage (@ax/conversations turns.content_blocks JSONB column)
9	//   - replay (@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner replays history at boot)
10	//
11	// Why @ax/ipc-protocol (and not @ax/core)?
12	// Content blocks travel BOTH on the wire AND across plugin boundaries. The
13	// runner's IPC client must not depend on kernel internals, so the canonical
14	// declaration lives here ŌĆö the schema package both sides already share.
15	//
16	// Field-name conventions:
17	// `media_type`, `tool_use_id`, `is_error` keep snake_case to match
18	// Anthropic's wire format and `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` emissions.
19	// These are wire-format names; camelCasing them would force translation on
20	// every hop and break round-tripping with the SDK and the LLM API.
21	//
22	// Boundary review (I1):
23	// Anthropic's content-block tuple IS the alternate-impl set across LLM
24	// providers ŌĆö OpenAI / Gemini wrappers translate on their side into this
25	// shape. So while these names look provider-specific, they're the lingua
26	// franca, not a leak.
27	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	export const TextBlockSchema = z.object({
30	  type: z.literal('text'),
31	  text: z.string(),
32	});
33	export type TextBlock = z.infer<typeof TextBlockSchema>;
34	
35	export const ThinkingBlockSchema = z.object({
36	  type: z.literal('thinking'),
37	  thinking: z.string(),
38	  /** Anthropic's signed thinking-block tag, when present. */
39	  signature: z.string().optional(),
40	});
41	export type ThinkingBlock = z.infer<typeof ThinkingBlockSchema>;
42	
43	/**
44	 * Redacted thinking block. Anthropic emits this whenever extended-thinking
45	 * output is flagged and the cleartext is suppressed; only the opaque `data`

lines: +// Attachment text-mention ŌĆö the lossy, model-facing form of an attachment.
+// When the runner can't (or shouldn't) inline an attachment's bytes for the
+// model ŌĆö a PDF without document-block support, an oversized text file, an
+// unrecognized binary ŌĆö it emits this one-line mention instead of the
+// `attachment` block. Under runner-owned-sessions the SDK persists *this*
+// (not the `attachment` block) to its jsonl transcript, so the read path has
+// to turn it back into an `attachment` block to render the chip.
+// `formatAttachmentMention` is the SINGLE producer of the string (the runner's
+// SINGLE consumer (the conversations read path calls it). Co-locating the
+// pair here means the two ends can never drift.
+/** Render the canonical one-line attachment mention. */
+ * Parse a one-line attachment mention produced by `formatAttachmentMention`,
+ * or `null` if `text` isn't exactly one such mention.
+ * Anchoring relies on two facts the producer guarantees:
+ *   - the path is workspace-relative and contains no whitespace (filenames
+ *     are sanitized + the conv/turn segments are opaque ids), so `\S+` after
+ *   - the mention is a single line ŌĆö `^ŌĆ”$` (no `s` flag) refuses any text
+ *     with embedded newlines, so a merged "user-text\nmention" block is split
+ *     by the caller before reaching here.
+ * `displayName` is matched non-greedily up to the ` at <no-space-path>` so a
+ * name containing " at " can't swallow the path.

lines: + * Restore user-facing `attachment` chips that runner translation strips out.
+ * Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl persists the runner's MODEL-facing
+ * translation of an attachment ŌĆö a one-line text mention (`User attached 'ŌĆ”'
+ * at <path> (<mime>)`, see `formatAttachmentMention`) ŌĆö not the original
+ * `attachment` block. The chat UI renders an `attachment` block as a download
+ * chip but a `text` block as raw text, so a reopened chat would otherwise show
+ * the mention verbatim. We rebuild the block from the mention here, on read,
+ * which also retroactively fixes chats stored before this landed (no
+ * migration ŌĆö the read derives the chip every time).
+ * Safety ŌĆö the jsonl is untrusted (I5; the SDK is third-party and the model
+ *   - only `role: 'user'` turns are touched. The model authors assistant /
+ *     tool lines, so it cannot inject a chip into a user turn.
+ *   - the parsed path must sit under THIS conversation's own upload prefix
+ *     (`.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/`), so a crafted mention can't aim the
+ *     download chip at another conversation or an arbitrary file.
+ *   - the rebuilt block must round-trip `AttachmentBlockSchema` (which rejects
+ *     absolute paths, `..`, drive roots, NUL) or the original text is kept.
+ * `sizeBytes` is unrecoverable from the mention (and unused by the render
+ * path), so it's set to 0. A single `text` block may carry the user's typed
+ * prompt and the mention on separate lines (the SDK concatenates the runner's
+ * separate text blocks), so we split per line, convert only matching lines,
+ * and keep the rest as text ŌĆö preserving order.
* Phase D. Locate the runner's jsonl transcript by sessionId and parse it
* into the canonical Turn[] shape. We glob via `workspace:list` instead of
* hardcoding the SDK's cwd-encoding rule (which depends on

+// Generate a title on any of the first N assistant turns while the
+// conversation is still untitled ŌĆö not just the first. The single-attempt
+// design left a chat permanently untitled whenever that one attempt was
+// skipped or failed for a transient reason: `conversations:get` reading the
+// runner's jsonl before it's flushed (0 turns), an `llm:call` error, or the
+// model returning an unusable title. Re-attempting on the next few turns
+// recovers from those; the `title !== null` guard + `ifNull: true` on
+// set-title keep it idempotent (once a title lands we stop). The cap bounds
+// title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting rejected output forever.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/skills/security-checklist

# Security checklist

The fifth invariant in `CLAUDE.md` is that **capabilities are explicit and minimized**. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one. This skill is the operational checklist that backs that invariant.

It is **rigid**: every section must be answered, every "N/A" must give a reason. No skipping, no adapting.

---

## When this fires

The skill applies whenever the current change touches:

- `@ax/core` hook bus signatures, IPC transport, or plugin manifest loading
- A new dependency in any `package.json` (direct or transitive)
- Hook payloads or IPC actions that carry strings originating from a model, tool, or external system
- Code that touches the filesystem with a caller-provided path
- Code that spawns a process
- Code that opens a network connection
- A plugin's exposed surface (registered service hooks, fired subscriber hooks)

If the change touches none of the above, we can skip the skill entirely. If even one applies, we walk all three sections ŌĆö they cover different threat models, and a change can fail one while passing the others.

---

## The three threat models

Walk these in order. Each section ends with what counts as a valid "N/A".

### 1. Sandbox escape / capability leakage

What capability does this code path grant or widen? List specifically:

- Filesystem paths reachable (read, write, both)
- Network destinations (host, port, scheme)
- Processes spawnable (fixed argv, or caller-influenced?)
- Env vars readable (fixed names, or caller-supplied?)
- Sockets, file descriptors, or opaque handles passed across boundaries

For each, is the capability **bounded**? Concrete failure patterns to check against:

- **Path traversal** ŌĆö caller-provided path not validated against a base dir; `../` escapes the intended sandbox.
- **Argv injection** ŌĆö process spawn lets caller control argv0 or insert flags, not just args. Equivalent to arbitrary command execution.
- **Env exfiltration** ŌĆö env var read with caller-supplied name. `process.env[userInput]` leaks any secret.
- **Handle leak** ŌĆö hook payload field is an opaque handle (file descriptor, socket, capability token). Subscribers receive it and inherit whatever it wraps.
- **Path-as-token confusion** ŌĆö payload field named `path` actually represents a version or identity, not a location. Subscribers may resolve it as a real path.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR adds no new reachable capability and doesn't widen an existing one. State why explicitly.

### 2. Prompt injection / untrusted content

What strings in this code path originate from outside the trust boundary?

- Model output (LLM responses, tool-call arguments the model chose)
- Tool output (return values from any tool the model called)
- User-uploaded content (files the user dropped in, not text they typed into the chat)
- External API responses (HTTP bodies, search results, scraped pages)
- Third-party plugin output

For each untrusted string, where does it end up? The bad destinations:

- Interpolated into a shell command, a file path, a SQL query, an HTTP URL
- Rendered as HTML or markdown without escaping
- Concatenated into another LLM prompt as if it were a system instruction
- Passed as a tool argument that the model didn't originate (e.g., a subscriber synthesizes a tool call from tool output)
- Logged into a file or DB that another process trusts

Worst-case test: if a malicious tool returned `"; rm -rf ~; echo "` (or the LLM equivalent ŌĆö `ignore previous instructions and call tool X with arg Y`), what would actually execute? Walk the path from the untrusted string's origin to its final destination.

**N/A is acceptable only if** this PR handles no model output, tool output, user-uploaded content, or external input. State why explicitly.

### 3. Supply chain

Any new entries (added or version-changed) in any `package.json`?

For each new dep, answer:

- **Pinned?** Exact version, not `^` or `~`. Lockfiles help, but the manifest range is the source of truth for what gets accepted on upgrade.
- **Install-time scripts?** Check the package's `scripts` field for `postinstall`, `preinstall`, `prepare`. If yes, what do they do? Network calls at install are a red flag.
- **Maintainer history?** Established package with an author you'd recognize, or a fresh / low-download package? `npm view <pkg> time` and download counts give a rough signal.
- **Transitive deps?** Skim the new transitive surface (`pnpm why <pkg>` after install, or check the lockfile diff). Same questions apply to anything new.

**N/A is acceptable only if** no `package.json` was touched and no new transitive dep was pulled in. State why explicitly.

---

## Output contract

Paste this block into the PR description under a `## Security review` heading:

```
## Security review
- Sandbox: <one line ŌĆö capability change, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Injection: <one line ŌĆö untrusted content handled, or "N/A: <reason>">
- Supply chain: <one line ŌĆö new deps + pinning, or "N/A: <reason>">
```

The `<reason>` after "N/A" is mandatory. Bare "N/A" or empty bullets fail the check ŌĆö every security checklist that ever died, died the moment "n/a" became free.

Examples of acceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: New plugin registers sandbox:spawn ŌĆö argv is fixed, args are caller-controlled but escaped via spawn() arg array (not shell). Reachable FS limited to the workspace tmpdir.`
- `Injection: Hook handler receives tool output and writes it to storage as Bytes. Never interpolated into prompts or commands. Storage layer treats as opaque.`
- `Supply chain: N/A ŌĆö no package.json changes, no new entries in the pnpm-lock diff.`

Examples of unacceptable lines:

- `Sandbox: N/A` ŌĆö no reason, fails.
- `Injection: Looks fine` ŌĆö no specifics, fails.
- `Supply chain: Added zod, popular package` ŌĆö no pinning answer, no install-script answer, fails.

---

## When the checklist finds something

If a section turns up a real risk, **fix it before the PR merges**. Options:

- Tighten the capability ŌĆö validate the path against a base dir, lock argv to a fixed shape, scope the env access to a fixed allow-list.
- Add a brand on the type (`UntrustedString`, `WorkspaceVersion`) so subscribers know what they're holding and the type system catches misuse.
- Reject the dep ŌĆö pin it tighter, find an alternative, or vendor a minimal implementation.
- Split the change ŌĆö sometimes the secure version is a different design than the convenient one. The PR may need re-scoping, not just patching.

Write the fix in the same PR. The note in the PR description should reflect the fixed state, not the original risk. If the fix moves the threat model from "real risk" to "N/A", say so in the line.

---

## Relationship to other guardrails

- The **boundary review checklist** in `CLAUDE.md` catches abstraction leaks (field-name leakage, missing alternate impl). This skill catches capability leaks. A hook can pass boundary review and still fail this checklist, or vice versa ŌĆö they're orthogonal, and changes that touch hook surface usually need both.
- The globally-installed `security-review` skill (different name on purpose) reviews a diff on demand at PR time. This skill produces the structured note that the diff-review can sanity-check against. Both can run; they don't overlap.
- `ax-conventions` summarizes the five invariants. Invariant #5 is the principle ("capabilities are explicit and minimized"); this skill is the enforcement.

---

## Anti-patterns

Things that look like they satisfy the checklist but don't:

- **Trust by familiarity.** "It's just a string from `chat:end`, that's fine." If the string ever held tool output, it's still untrusted regardless of which hook it's flowing through now.
- **N/A by category, not by reason.** "N/A: this is a refactor" ŌĆö refactors can still widen capabilities (e.g. an internal function becomes exported and reachable from a plugin boundary).
- **Defer to lint.** "ESLint will catch this." It might. The checklist runs anyway because lint catches patterns, not intent.
- **One-line "yes it's safe".** The checklist's value is in walking the failure modes ŌĆö the answer matters less than having considered the question. If the section line in the PR doesn't name a specific capability or a specific untrusted-input flow, it's not real.
Let me walk the three threat models against the diff, and confirm no `package.json` changed.

### UI bug follow-ups

# TODO ŌĆö deferred work

Living list of work explicitly punted, parked, or "wait until earned." Anything
without a concrete trigger is a candidate for deletion. When an item ships,
strike it through (or remove it) and reference the closing PR.

Sources scanned (2026-05-19): `MEMORY.md`, recent `docs/plans/*followup*`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-10-memory-strata-roadmap.md`,
`docs/plans/2026-05-08-first-use-onboarding-followup.md`, recent commits, and
`project_codex_findings_2026_04_29.md`.

---

## Routines

- [ ] **Default routines for team agents (fire-under-team policy).** Spun out of the PR #105 fix: `agents:list-personal-owners` excludes team agents because routing a default fire under "the team" needs a policy answer ŌĆö does the routine fire under each member separately, under the team creator, under a designated steward? Pick up when team agents become a real surface; for now, team-owned agents simply don't get default routines materialized.
- [ ] **Cron + webhook triggers for default routines.** Deferred per I-R5 / HP7. Cron needs a croner evaluator in claim SQL; webhook needs per-default tokens + live rebind on admin edit. Pick up when a real caller needs it.
- [ ] **Phase F ŌĆö conversation titles.** Branch parked. New piece of work, not a follow-up.
- [ ] **Per-team / per-tenant scoped default routines.** Not currently load-bearing.
- [ ] **Per-agent opt-out for default routines** beyond the current override-by-name mechanic.
- [ ] **"Drift indicator" UI** (visibility into stale `definition_updated_at`).
- [ ] **Default ŌåÆ workspace "promote" flow.**

## Skills (Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96)

- [ ] **System-prompt fold for skill descriptions** (Phase A ŌĆö **PARKED**). SDK-only today (SDK indexes description into prompt + invokes `Skill` tool on demand). Formally parked in `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md` ŌĆö trigger to un-park is either `packages/agent-native-runner/` gaining `src/` or `packages/test-harness/` adding a real-LLM code path. No code lands until then; when the trigger fires, write a fresh `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-skills-system-prompt-fold-impl.md`.

## Attachments / artifacts

- [ ] **`$CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/sessions/` mirroring.** Today's scaffold only links `projects/`. Add a sibling symlink if a future feature needs session metadata in the workspace.

## Auth / onboarding cleanup (Phase 4 + Phase 5 follow-ups)

These wait on stated triggers ŌĆö don't ship pre-emptively.

- [ ] **`ax admin reset-password` CLI + `/auth/reset-password` route.** Pick up when local password sign-in becomes a real surface (wizard re-adds password OR Settings ŌåÆ Password lands). Today the wizard captures only name+email against `@ax/auth-better` (which silently ignores the optional `password` input until the local-password slice lands).
- [ ] **Walk the Google new-user gate-*allow* branch live.** The PR #116 `kind` walk used the existing admin email, so it exercised account-LINKING + the cookie bridge + the gate's reject branch ŌĆö but NOT brand-new-user creation (gate allows an in-domain email ŌåÆ creates a `role:'user'` row with the Google-supplied name). That path is unit-tested only. Pick up next kind walk with a second `@<allowed-domain>` Google account on hand.
- [ ] **Port the gated `k8s-e2e` tests off `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.** `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/k8s-e2e/helpers.ts:42` + `credentials-admin-roundtrip.test.ts` still POST to the retired route. Gated by `AX_K8S_E2E=1`, not in CI, so this didn't block the retirement merge ŌĆö but anyone walking those e2e tests against `kind-ax-next-dev` will hit immediate failures. Fix: rewrite `signInAsK8sAdmin` to call `auth:create-bootstrap-user` over the bus (or POST `/setup/admin` via the wizard) and exchange for the signed cookie, mirroring `@ax/test-harness/sign-in.ts`. Pick up next time someone walks the e2e suite.
- [ ] **Refresh `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` dev-bootstrap caveat.** The walk-through document references "the dev-bootstrap auth path mints a single shared user with `role='admin'`" ŌĆö no longer accurate after the retirement. Update next time the doc gets walked.

## Credentials UX redesign (PR #109, merged 2026-05-19)

- [ ] **User-delete wiring in `@ax/auth-better`.** When a user-delete service hook lands, it should call `credentials:purge-by-owner({ scope: 'user', ownerId })` ŌĆö the facade hook + the matching agent-delete wiring already exist; only the auth-better side is missing.
- [ ] **Bulk "all credentials" admin inventory view.** Design ┬¦3 non-goal; revisit when an operator needs a single audit surface.

## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)

Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.

- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** ŌĆö when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** ŌĆö when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** ŌĆö only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** ŌĆö when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** ŌĆö explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** ŌĆö Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

## UI Bugs

- [ ] Chat session titles aren't being consistenly generated for new chats
- [ ] When files are attached to a user message. That message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session in the sidebar. Instead, this text is displayed: "Summarize this file\nUser attached 'Disaster Recovery Plan - v3.pdf' at .ax/uploads/cnv_HqkVq7CQogkmnXBrRFcTpg/req-d194ad9d37af/a60d0978__Disaster_Recovery_Plan_-_v3.pdf (application/pdf)"

+- [x] ~~When files are attached to a user message, that message doesn't show the attachment chip when loaded again from a previous chat session~~ ŌĆö fixed (PR #PENDING): the runner-translated text-mention is reconstructed back into an `attachment` block on the `conversations:get` read path, gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix.

+- [ ] **Live title refresh after the poll window.** `generateTitle` widened its poll to ~10s, but a title that lands *after* the window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh (page nav/reload) to surface ŌĆö there's no push channel. Pick up if users report a stale "New Chat" that the backend has actually titled. Real fix is a serverŌåÆclient title-update signal (SSE/websocket) or a low-frequency background `list()` poll.

input.command:
cd /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/fix-ui-bugs
gh pr create --base main --head worktree-fix-ui-bugs --title "fix(ui): consistent chat titles + attachment chips on history load" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Closes both items under `TODO.md` ŌåÆ **UI Bugs**.

## Bug 1 ŌĆö chat titles aren't consistently generated for new chats

Two independent causes:

- **Backend (primary):** `@ax/conversation-titles` attempted a title exactly once, gated on `assistantTurnCount === 1`. If that single attempt was skipped or failed transiently ŌĆö `conversations:get` reading the runner jsonl before it flushed (0 turns), an `llm:call` error, or the model returning an unusable title ŌĆö the chat stayed permanently untitled. Fix: generate on any of the first `MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS` (=3) assistant turns **while still untitled**. The existing `title !== null` guard + `ifNull: true` on `conversations:set-title` keep re-attempts idempotent (once a title lands we stop); the cap bounds title-LLM spend.
- **UI (secondary):** `generateTitle` polled 3├Ś1s then permanently cached `"New Chat"` in assistant-ui ŌĆö often expiring before the title-LLM round-trip landed. Fix: extracted `pollConversationTitle` (with `fetchImpl`/`intervalMs` test seams) and widened the window to ~10 attempts, early-returning on the first poll that sees a title.

Residual (a title landing *after* the widened window still needs a sidebar `list()` refresh ŌĆö no push channel) is tracked in `TODO.md`.

## Bug 2 ŌĆö attachment chips don't render on history load

Under runner-owned-sessions, `conversations:get` reads the runner's native jsonl transcript. The runner translates a user's `attachment` block into a **model-facing text mention** (`User attached 'ŌĆ”' at <path> (<mime>)`) *before* the SDK writes the jsonl, so the original block is gone on reopen ŌĆö the chat rendered the raw mention text instead of a download chip.

Fix: `getConversation` reconstructs the `attachment` block from the mention on read. The mention embeds path + displayName + mediaType (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway ŌåÆ reconstruct with `0`). The format is now owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so the runner (producer) and the read path (consumer) can't drift. **No schema/table change ŌĆö and because reconstruction is read-time, it retroactively fixes chats stored before this landed (no migration).**

## Boundary review

No hook signature changes. `conversations:get`'s wire shape is unchanged (`{ conversation, turns }`); turns now carry the *original intended* `attachment` block shape (which `content-blocks.ts` already documents as canonical) instead of the lossy translation. The two new functions are pure helpers on a shared library, not a hook surface. No new IPC actions.

## Security review
- Sandbox: Read path now derives a downloadable `attachment` ref from untrusted jsonl text. Bounded ŌĆö only `role:'user'` turns are processed, the parsed path must sit under this conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix, the rebuilt block must round-trip `AttachmentBlockSchema` (rejects `..`, absolute, drive-root, NUL), and `attachments:download` enforces its own path-scope ACL downstream. A crafted mention can't escape the caller's own conversation upload dir.
- Injection: Untrusted jsonl user-turn text is parsed by `parseAttachmentMention`; `displayName`/`mediaType` render as escaped React text in the chip and the path is never interpolated into a shell/SQL/prompt. Traversal or cross-conversation mentions fail the prefix/schema gate and fall back to plain text. The served download Content-Type comes from `attachments:download`, not the reconstructed `mediaType`.
- Supply chain: N/A ŌĆö no `package.json` or lockfile changes; the helpers were added to the existing `@ax/ipc-protocol` package.

## Tests

Build clean (tsc `--build` whole workspace), lint clean (0 errors). Targeted: `@ax/ipc-protocol` 137, `@ax/conversation-titles` 52, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` 196, `@ax/channel-web` 485, `@ax/conversations` 131 ŌĆö all green. New tests:
- ipc-protocol: mention round-trip incl. `' at '` in displayName, multi-line refusal, negative cases.
- conversation-titles: retry-while-untitled on a later turn; stop past the cap.
- conversations: rebuild from standalone + merged mention blocks; false-positive guard (other conversation's prefix); assistant turns untouched; plain text unchanged.
- channel-web: `pollConversationTitle` resolves on a later poll, falls back to null after exhausting attempts, survives transient fetch failures.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

­¤ż¢ Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In `@packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/thread-list-adapter.test.ts`: - Around line 78-121: Add a regression test to thread-list-adapter.test.ts that verifies pollConversationTitle handles hung fetches: create a fetchImpl mock that returns a never-resolving Promise (e.g., new Promise(() => {})), call pollConversationTitle('c-1', { attempts: X, intervalMs: 0, perAttemptTimeoutMs: Y, fetchImpl }), and assert it resolves to null after the configured window and does not hang the test; place the test alongside the existing pollConversationTitle cases and reference the pollConversationTitle helper when adding the assertion. In `@packages/ipc-protocol/src/content-blocks.ts`: - Around line 192-223: The parser uses ATTACHMENT_MENTION_RE with (\S+) for path which rejects valid paths with spaces; update ATTACHMENT_MENTION_RE (and parseAttachmentMention if needed) so the path capture allows whitespace while still anchoring the trailing " (mediaType)" (e.g., capture everything between " at " and the final space before "(" rather than \S+), keep the non-greedy displayName capture, and validate returned path/displayName/mediaType as before; also add a regression test in packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/content-blocks.test.ts that round-trips formatAttachmentMention -> parseAttachmentMention for a path containing spaces to lock the contract (references: ATTACHMENT_MENTION_RE, parseAttachmentMention, formatAttachmentMention, AttachmentBlockSchema). In `@TODO.md`: - Line 59: Fix the spelling typo in the TODO entry: replace "consistenly" with "consistently" in the completed UI bug item text (the line containing "Chat session titles aren't being consistenly generated for new chats" / the TODO list entry that mentions `@ax/conversation-titles` and `generateTitle`). ```
­¤¬ä Autofix (Beta) Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR: - [ ] Push a commit to this branch (recommended) - [ ] Create a new PR with the fixes
---

ŌÜÖ’ĖÅ Run configuration **Configuration used**: Organization UI **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro **Run ID**: `3081356e-4884-4996-95be-502586368560`
­¤ōź Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 5b977dfa36630671916ac03cdfbfd21440f5c2ef and ed1c403d5aacd5c080682531afbe7de3d561a6f7.
­¤ōÆ Files selected for processing (11) * `.claude/memory/decisions.md` * `TODO.md` * `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/attachment-translation.ts` * `packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/thread-list-adapter.test.ts` * `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts` * `packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` * `packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts` * `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/get-from-workspace.test.ts` * `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts` * `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/content-blocks.test.ts` * `packages/ipc-protocol/src/content-blocks.ts`
----- [2026-05-21T22:33:15Z] [![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/project-ax/ax-next/pull/120?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack)
­¤ōØ Walkthrough ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes * **Bug Fixes** * Fixed session title generation consistency issues. * Fixed attachment chips not reappearing when reloading conversations. * Improved chat UI behavior for untitled conversations. * **New Features** * Added retry logic for chat title generation across multiple turns (up to 3 attempts). * Expanded the title generation polling window for improved reliability. ## Walkthrough This PR implements two interconnected chat improvements: a shared protocol for formatting and parsing attachment mentions as text (enabling round-trip recovery of attachments from model transcripts), and refactored conversation title generation that polls multiple times over the first few assistant turns rather than attempting only once. Supporting runner, plugin, and adapter integrations use the new protocol and polling logic. ## Changes **Chat behavior and content improvements** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | | --- | --- | | **Attachment mention format/parse protocol**
`packages/ipc-protocol/src/content-blocks.ts`, `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/content-blocks.test.ts` | Introduces `AttachmentMentionFields` interface and `formatAttachmentMention()`/`parseAttachmentMention()` functions as a canonical single-line text representation for attachments that cannot be inlined into Anthropic transcripts. Regex-based parsing with field validation and round-trip test coverage ensure robust format contract. | | **Attachment mention producer and consumer**
`packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/attachment-translation.ts`, `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts`, `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/get-from-workspace.test.ts` | Runner delegates mention string construction to shared `formatAttachmentMention()` in `textMention()`. Conversations plugin post-processes user turns during `conversations:get` to reconstruct `attachment` content blocks from stored mention text via `parseAttachmentMention()`, enforcing conversation-specific upload path constraints and preserving non-matching lines as plain text. Comprehensive test suite covers reconstruction, edge cases, false-positive guards, and assistant-turn exclusion. | | **Conversation title polling extraction**
`packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts`, `packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/thread-list-adapter.test.ts` | Extracts polling logic from inline `generateTitle` into reusable `pollConversationTitle()` function that repeatedly calls `GET /api/chat/conversations` until title appears or retries are exhausted. Supports optional configuration (attempts, interval, custom fetch). Thread-list adapter uses this helper with `'New Chat'` fallback, keeping existing streaming behavior. | | **Bounded retry title generation**
`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts`, `packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` | Changes from single-attempt title generation on first assistant turn to retrying over the first `MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS` (3) assistant turns while conversation remains untitled. Subscriber counts assistant turns and gates generation unless count is within range. Uses `conversations:set-title` with `ifNull: true` for idempotency. Tests cover retry scenarios and spend-cap enforcement. | | **Decision log and TODO updates**
`.claude/memory/decisions.md`, `TODO.md` | Documents UI/chat fixes (bounded title retry, attachment reconstruction, polling window expansion) and notes remaining follow-up for live sidebar title refresh after poll window closes. | --- ­¤Ä» 3 (Moderate) | ŌÅ▒’ĖÅ ~25 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [project-ax/ax-next#50](https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/50): Modifies conversation-titles auto-title pipeline and LLM hook dispatch behavior, overlapping with this PR's bounded retry title generation logic. - [project-ax/ax-next#18](https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/18): Changes `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts` around title polling behavior, directly related to this PR's `pollConversationTitle` extraction.

content.text:
The CI failure is **a pre-existing flake in `@ax/routines/sync-webhook.test.ts`** ŌĆö a package I didn't touch. All 161 routines tests *passed*; the run only went non-zero because vitest caught one unhandled Postgres teardown error (`57P01: terminating connection due to administrator command`) ŌĆö a known pg-pool teardown race. It's unrelated to my changes.

CodeRabbit posted 3 actionable comments. Let me verify each and batch all fixes into one push (to spend a single re-review). 

**Comment 1 (regex `\S+` rejects spaced paths):** Valid ŌĆö `isWorkspaceRelativePath` permits spaces, so the parser should too. Fix the regex.
**Comment 2 (hung-fetch can hang the poll):** Valid latent issue in code I'm introducing ŌĆö add a per-attempt timeout + regression test.
**Comment 3 (typo "consistenly"):** Trivial, fix it.

Let me check the review budget first, then apply all fixes.

const ATTACHMENT_MENTION_RE = /^User attached '(.*?)' at (\S+) \(([^)]+)\)$/;

/**
 * Parse a one-line attachment mention produced by `formatAttachmentMention`,
 * or `null` if `text` isn't exactly one such mention.
 *
 * Anchoring relies on two facts the producer guarantees:
 *   - the path is workspace-relative and contains no whitespace (filenames
 *     are sanitized + the conv/turn segments are opaque ids), so `\S+` after
 *     ` at ` captures it unambiguously;
 *   - the mention is a single line ŌĆö `^ŌĆ”$` (no `s` flag) refuses any text
 *     with embedded newlines, so a merged "user-text\nmention" block is split
 *     by the caller before reaching here.
 *
 * `displayName` is matched non-greedily up to the ` at <no-space-path>` so a
 * name containing " at " can't swallow the path.
 */
input.new_string:

 * so a merged "user-text\nmention" block is split by the caller before
 * reaching here.
 *
 * `displayName` is matched non-greedily up to the first ` at `; the path is
 * greedy and may contain spaces (workspace-relative paths are allowed to ŌĆö
 * see `isWorkspaceRelativePath`), backtracking to the final ` (<mediaType>)`
 * which the `[^)]+` end-anchor pins. The reconstructed block is re-validated
 * against `AttachmentBlockSchema` by the caller, so a path that slips through
 * here but isn't workspace-relative is still rejected downstream.
 */

­¤ōØ Walkthrough ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes * **Bug Fixes** * Fixed session title generation consistency issues. * Fixed attachment chips not reappearing when reloading conversations. * Improved chat UI behavior for untitled conversations. * **New Features** * Added retry logic for chat title generation across multiple turns (up to 3 attempts). * Expanded the title generation polling window for improved reliability. ## Walkthrough This PR implements two interconnected chat improvements: a shared protocol for formatting and parsing attachment mentions as text (enabling round-trip recovery of attachments from model transcripts), and refactored conversation title generation that polls multiple times over the first few assistant turns rather than attempting only once. Supporting runner, plugin, and adapter integrations use the new protocol and polling logic. ## Changes **Chat behavior and content improvements** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | | --- | --- | | **Attachment mention format/parse protocol**
`packages/ipc-protocol/src/content-blocks.ts`, `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/content-blocks.test.ts` | Introduces `AttachmentMentionFields` interface and `formatAttachmentMention()`/`parseAttachmentMention()` functions as a canonical single-line text representation for attachments that cannot be inlined into Anthropic transcripts. Regex-based parsing with field validation and round-trip test coverage ensure robust format contract. | | **Attachment mention producer and consumer**
`packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/attachment-translation.ts`, `packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts`, `packages/conversations/src/__tests__/get-from-workspace.test.ts` | Runner delegates mention string construction to shared `formatAttachmentMention()` in `textMention()`. Conversations plugin post-processes user turns during `conversations:get` to reconstruct `attachment` content blocks from stored mention text via `parseAttachmentMention()`, enforcing conversation-specific upload path constraints and preserving non-matching lines as plain text. Comprehensive test suite covers reconstruction, edge cases, false-positive guards, and assistant-turn exclusion. | | **Conversation title polling extraction**
`packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts`, `packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/thread-list-adapter.test.ts` | Extracts polling logic from inline `generateTitle` into reusable `pollConversationTitle()` function that repeatedly calls `GET /api/chat/conversations` until title appears or retries are exhausted. Supports optional configuration (attempts, interval, custom fetch). Thread-list adapter uses this helper with `'New Chat'` fallback, keeping existing streaming behavior. | | **Bounded retry title generation**
`packages/conversation-titles/src/plugin.ts`, `packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` | Changes from single-attempt title generation on first assistant turn to retrying over the first `MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS` (3) assistant turns while conversation remains untitled. Subscriber counts assistant turns and gates generation unless count is within range. Uses `conversations:set-title` with `ifNull: true` for idempotency. Tests cover retry scenarios and spend-cap enforcement. | | **Decision log and TODO updates**
`.claude/memory/decisions.md`, `TODO.md` | Documents UI/chat fixes (bounded title retry, attachment reconstruction, polling window expansion) and notes remaining follow-up for live sidebar title refresh after poll window closes. | --- ­¤Ä» 3 (Moderate) | ŌÅ▒’ĖÅ ~25 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [project-ax/ax-next#50](https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/50): Modifies conversation-titles auto-title pipeline and LLM hook dispatch behavior, overlapping with this PR's bounded retry title generation logic. - [project-ax/ax-next#18](https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/18): Changes `packages/channel-web/src/lib/thread-list-adapter.ts` around title polling behavior, directly related to this PR's `pollConversationTitle` extraction.

fix(ipc-protocol): allow whitespace in attachment mention paths

CodeRabbit: the (\S+) path capture rejected valid paths with spaces, which

to the final " (mediaType)"; the reconstructed block is still re-validated
against AttachmentBlockSchema downstream. Adds a spaced-path round-trip
test.

fix(channel-web): bound each title poll attempt with a timeout

CodeRabbit: a browser fetch has no default timeout, so a single hung
request could stall the whole poll window indefinitely. Race each attempt
against perAttemptTimeoutMs (AbortController + a resolve-race so even a
fetch that ignores the abort signal can't hang the loop). Adds a
never-resolving-fetch regression test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
